127	    def _handle_dtype_to_regex(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
128	        if sampling_params.dtype is None:
129	            return
130	
131	        if sampling_params.stop == ():
132	            sampling_params.stop = []
133	
134	        dtype_regex = None
135	        if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:
136	
137	            dtype_regex = REGEX_INT
138	            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
139	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:
140	
141	            dtype_regex = REGEX_FLOAT
142	            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
143	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["str", str]:
144	
145	            dtype_regex = REGEX_STR
146	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["bool", bool]:
147	
148	            dtype_regex = REGEX_BOOL
149	        else:
150	            raise RuntimeError(f"Invalid dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}")
151	
152	        if dtype_regex is not None and sampling_params.regex is not None:
153	            warnings.warn(
154	                f"Both dtype and regex are set. Only dtype will be used. dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}, regex: {sampling_params.regex}"
155	            )
156	
157	        sampling_params.regex = dtype_regex
158	
159	    def generate(
160	        self,
161	        s: StreamExecutor,
162	        sampling_params: SglSamplingParams,
163	    ):
164	        self._handle_dtype_to_regex(sampling_params)
165	        data = {
166	            "text": s.text_,
167	            "sampling_params": {
168	                "skip_special_tokens": global_config.skip_special_tokens_in_output,
169	                "spaces_between_special_tokens": global_config.spaces_between_special_tokens_in_out,
170	                **sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs(),
171	            },
172	        }
173	
174	        for item in [
175	            "return_logprob",
176	            "logprob_start_len",
177	            "top_logprobs_num",
178	            "return_text_in_logprobs",
179	        ]:
180	            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
181	            if value is not None:
182	                data[item] = value
183	
184	        self._add_images(s, data)
185	
186	        res = http_request(
187	            self.base_url + "/generate",
188	            json=data,
189	            api_key=self.api_key,
190	            verify=self.verify,
191	        )
192	        self._assert_success(res)
193	
194	        obj = res.json()
195	        comp = obj["text"]
196	        return comp, obj["meta_info"]
197	
198	    def generate_stream(
199	        self,
200	        s: StreamExecutor,
201	        sampling_params: SglSamplingParams,
202	    ):
203	        self._handle_dtype_to_regex(sampling_params)
204	
205	        data = {
206	            "text": s.text_,
207	            "sampling_params": {
208	                "skip_special_tokens": global_config.skip_special_tokens_in_output,
209	                "spaces_between_special_tokens": global_config.spaces_between_special_tokens_in_out,
210	                **sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs(),
211	            },
212	        }
213	
214	        for item in [
215	            "return_logprob",
216	            "logprob_start_len",
217	            "top_logprobs_num",
218	            "return_text_in_logprobs",
219	        ]:
220	            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
221	            if value is not None:
222	                data[item] = value
223	
224	        data["stream"] = True
225	        self._add_images(s, data)
226	
227	        res = http_request(
228	            self.base_url + "/generate",
229	            json=data,
230	            stream=True,
231	            api_key=self.api_key,
232	            verify=self.verify,
233	        )
234	        self._assert_success(res)
235	        pos = 0
236	
237	        for chunk in res.iter_lines(decode_unicode=False):
238	            chunk = chunk.decode("utf-8")
239	            if chunk and chunk.startswith("data:"):
240	                if chunk == "data: [DONE]":
241	                    break
242	                data = json.loads(chunk[5:].strip("\n"))
243	                chunk_text = data["text"][pos:]
244	                meta_info = data["meta_info"]
245	                pos += len(chunk_text)
246	                yield chunk_text, meta_info
247	
248	    def select(
249	        self,
250	        s: StreamExecutor,
251	        choices: List[str],
252	        temperature: float,
253	        choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod,
254	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
255	        assert temperature <= 1e-5
256	
257	        # Cache common prefix
258	        data = {"text": s.text_, "sampling_params": {"max_new_tokens": 0}}
259	        obj = self._generate_http_request(s, data)
260	        prompt_len = obj["meta_info"]["prompt_tokens"]
261	        logprob_start_len = max(prompt_len - 2, 0)  # For token healing
262	
263	        # Compute logprob
264	        data = {
265	            "text": [s.text_ + c for c in choices],
266	            "sampling_params": {
267	                "max_new_tokens": 0,
268	                "temperature": 0,
269	            },
270	            "return_logprob": True,
271	            "return_text_in_logprobs": True,
272	            "logprob_start_len": logprob_start_len,
273	        }
274	        obj = self._generate_http_request(s, data)
275	
276	        input_token_logprobs = [r["meta_info"]["input_token_logprobs"] for r in obj]
277	        output_token_logprobs = [r["meta_info"]["output_token_logprobs"] for r in obj]
278	        normalized_prompt_logprobs = [
279	            compute_normalized_prompt_logprobs(r["meta_info"]["input_token_logprobs"])
280	            for r in obj
281	        ]
282	
283	        # Remove extra token if no token healing occurred
284	        for i in range(len(input_token_logprobs)):
285	            healed_token_str = input_token_logprobs[i][0][-1]
286	            if s.text_.endswith(healed_token_str):
287	                healed_token_logprob = input_token_logprobs[i][0][0]
288	                normalized_prompt_logprobs[i] = (
289	                    normalized_prompt_logprobs[i] * len(input_token_logprobs[i])
290	                    - healed_token_logprob
291	                ) / (len(input_token_logprobs[i]) - 1)
292	                input_token_logprobs[i] = input_token_logprobs[i][1:]
293	
294	        # Compute unconditional logprobs if required
295	        if choices_method.requires_unconditional_logprobs:
296	            input_ids = [[el[1] for el in subl] for subl in input_token_logprobs]
297	            data = {
298	                "input_ids": input_ids,
299	                "sampling_params": {"max_new_tokens": 0},
300	                "return_logprob": True,
301	            }
302	            obj = self._generate_http_request(s, data)
303	            unconditional_token_logprobs = [
304	                r["meta_info"]["input_token_logprobs"] for r in obj
305	            ]
306	        else:
307	            unconditional_token_logprobs = None
308	
309	        return choices_method(
310	            choices=choices,
311	            normalized_prompt_logprobs=normalized_prompt_logprobs,
312	            input_token_logprobs=input_token_logprobs,
313	            output_token_logprobs=output_token_logprobs,
314	            unconditional_token_logprobs=unconditional_token_logprobs,
315	        )
316	
317	    def concatenate_and_append(self, src_rids: List[str], dst_rid: str):
318	        res = http_request(
319	            self.base_url + "/concate_and_append_request",
320	            json={"src_rids": src_rids, "dst_rid": dst_rid},
321	            api_key=self.api_key,
322	            verify=self.verify,
323	        )
324	        self._assert_success(res)
325	
326	    def _generate_http_request(self, s: StreamExecutor, data):
327	        self._add_images(s, data)
328	        res = http_request(
329	            self.base_url + "/generate",
330	            json=data,
331	            api_key=self.api_key,
332	            verify=self.verify,
333	        )
334	        self._assert_success(res)
335	        return res.json()
336	
337	    def _add_images(self, s: StreamExecutor, data):
338	        if s.images_:
339	            assert len(s.images_) == 1, "Only support one image."
340	            data["image_data"] = s.images_[0][1]
341	
342	    def _assert_success(self, res):
343	        if res.status_code != 200:
344	            try:
345	                content = res.json()
346	            except json.JSONDecodeError:
347	                content = res.text
348	            raise RuntimeError(content)
349	
350	
351	def compute_normalized_prompt_logprobs(input_logprobs):
352	    values = [x[0] for x in input_logprobs if x[0]]
353	    return sum(values) / len(values)

356	class Runtime:
357	    """
358	    A wrapper for the HTTP server.
359	    This is used for launching the server in a python program without
360	    using the command line interface.
361	
362	    It is mainly used for the frontend language.
363	    You should use the Engine class if you want to do normal offline processing without the frontend language.
364	    """
365	
366	    def __init__(
367	        self,
368	        log_level: str = "error",
369	        launch_timeout: float = 300.0,
370	        *args,
371	        **kwargs,
372	    ):
373	        """See the arguments in server_args.py::ServerArgs
374	
375	        Args:
376	            log_level: Log level for the server.
377	            timeout: Timeout in seconds for waiting for the server to start.
378	            *args: Additional arguments passed to ServerArgs.
379	            **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments passed to ServerArgs.
380	        """
381	        # We delay the import of any `sglang.srt` components in `sglang.lang`, so users can run
382	        # client code without installing SRT server and its dependency if they want.
383	        from sglang.srt.entrypoints.http_server import launch_server
384	        from sglang.srt.server_args import ServerArgs
385	        from sglang.srt.utils import is_port_available
386	
387	        self.server_args = ServerArgs(*args, log_level=log_level, **kwargs)
388	
389	        # Pre-allocate ports
390	        for port in range(self.server_args.port, 40000):
391	            if is_port_available(port):
392	                break
393	        self.server_args.port = port
394	
395	        self.url = self.server_args.url()
396	        self.generate_url = self.url + "/generate"
397	
398	        # NOTE: We store pid instead of proc to fix some issues during __delete__
399	        self.pid = None
400	
401	        ctx = multiprocessing.get_context("spawn")
402	        proc = ctx.Process(
403	            target=launch_server,
404	            args=(self.server_args,),
405	        )
406	        proc.start()
407	        self.pid = proc.pid
408	
409	        # Before python program terminates, call shutdown implicitly. Therefore, users don't have to explicitly call .shutdown()
410	        atexit.register(self.shutdown)
411	
412	        # Wait for server to be ready by polling /health_generate
413	        start_time = time.time()
414	        with requests.Session() as session:
415	            while time.time() - start_time < launch_timeout:
416	                try:
417	                    response = session.get(f"{self.url}/health_generate")
418	                    if response.status_code == 200:
419	                        break
420	                except requests.RequestException:
421	                    pass
422	
423	                if not proc.is_alive():
424	                    self.shutdown()
425	                    raise RuntimeError(
426	                        "Initialization failed. Please see the error messages above."
427	                    )
428	
429	                time.sleep(2)
430	            else:
431	                self.shutdown()
432	                raise TimeoutError("Server failed to start within the timeout period.")
433	
434	        self.endpoint = RuntimeEndpoint(self.url)
435	
436	    def shutdown(self):
437	        from sglang.srt.utils import kill_process_tree
438	
439	        if self.pid is not None:
440	            kill_process_tree(self.pid)
441	            self.pid = None
442	
443	    def start_profile(self):
444	        self.endpoint.start_profile()
445	
446	    def stop_profile(self):
447	        self.endpoint.stop_profile()
448	
449	    def cache_prefix(self, prefix: str):
450	        self.endpoint.cache_prefix(prefix)
451	
452	    def get_tokenizer(self):
453	        from sglang.srt.utils.hf_transformers_utils import get_tokenizer
454	
455	        return get_tokenizer(
456	            self.server_args.tokenizer_path,
457	            tokenizer_mode=self.server_args.tokenizer_mode,
458	            trust_remote_code=self.server_args.trust_remote_code,
459	            revision=self.server_args.revision,
460	        )
461	
462	    async def async_generate(
463	        self,
464	        prompt: str,
465	        sampling_params: Optional[Dict] = None,
466	    ):
467	        if self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
468	            json_data = {
469	                "input_ids": prompt,
470	                "sampling_params": sampling_params,
471	                "stream": True,
472	            }
473	        else:
474	            json_data = {
475	                "text": prompt,
476	                "sampling_params": sampling_params,
477	                "stream": True,
478	            }
479	        pos = 0
480	
481	        timeout = aiohttp.ClientTimeout(total=3 * 3600)
482	        async with aiohttp.ClientSession(timeout=timeout, trust_env=True) as session:
483	            async with session.post(self.generate_url, json=json_data) as response:
484	                async for chunk, _ in response.content.iter_chunks():
485	                    chunk = chunk.decode("utf-8")
486	                    if chunk and chunk.startswith("data:"):
487	                        if chunk == "data: [DONE]\n\n":
488	                            break
489	                        data = json.loads(chunk[5:].strip("\n"))
490	                        if "text" in data:
491	                            cur = data["text"][pos:]
492	                            if cur:
493	                                yield cur
494	                            pos += len(cur)
495	                        else:
496	                            yield data
497	
498	    add_request = async_generate
499	
500	    def generate(
501	        self,
502	        prompt: Union[str, List[str]],
503	        sampling_params: Optional[Dict] = None,
504	        return_logprob: Optional[Union[List[bool], bool]] = False,
505	        logprob_start_len: Optional[Union[List[int], int]] = None,
506	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[Union[List[int], int]] = None,
507	        lora_path: Optional[List[Optional[str]]] = None,
508	    ):
509	        json_data = {
510	            "text": prompt,
511	            "sampling_params": sampling_params,
512	            "return_logprob": return_logprob,
513	            "logprob_start_len": logprob_start_len,
514	            "top_logprobs_num": top_logprobs_num,
515	            "lora_path": lora_path,
516	        }
517	        assert not isinstance(lora_path, list) or len(lora_path) == len(prompt)
518	        response = requests.post(
519	            self.url + "/generate",
520	            json=json_data,
521	        )
522	        return json.dumps(response.json())
523	
524	    def encode(
525	        self,
526	        prompt: Union[str, List[str], List[Dict], List[List[Dict]]],
527	    ):
528	        json_data = {"text": prompt}
529	        response = requests.post(self.url + "/encode", json=json_data)
530	        return json.dumps(response.json())
531	
532	    async def get_server_info(self):
533	        async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
534	            async with session.get(f"{self.url}/get_server_info") as response:
535	                if response.status == 200:
536	                    return await response.json()
537	                else:
538	                    error_data = await response.json()
539	                    raise RuntimeError(
540	                        f"Failed to get server info. {error_data['error']['message']}"
541	                    )
542	
543	    def __del__(self):
544	        self.shutdown()

149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)

151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()

199	GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}
200	
201	
202	def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
203	    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func
204	
205	
206	def create_grammar_backend(
207	    server_args: ServerArgs,
208	    tokenizer,
209	    vocab_size: int,
210	    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
211	) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
212	    name = server_args.grammar_backend
213	
214	    # Custom grammar backend has the highest priority
215	    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
216	        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](
217	            server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids
218	        )
219	
220	    # Default grammar backends
221	    if name == "outlines":
222	        from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import OutlinesGrammarBackend
223	
224	        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(
225	            tokenizer,
226	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
227	        )
228	    elif name == "xgrammar":
229	        from sglang.srt.constrained.xgrammar_backend import (
230	            TokenizerNotSupportedError,
231	            XGrammarGrammarBackend,
232	        )
233	
234	        # Convert Set[int] to List[int] if needed
235	        eos_list = list(eos_token_ids) if eos_token_ids else None
236	
237	        try:
238	            grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
239	                tokenizer,
240	                vocab_size=vocab_size,
241	                model_eos_token_ids=eos_list,
242	                any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
243	            )
244	        except TokenizerNotSupportedError as e:
245	            logger.warning(
246	                f"Grammar backend disabled because tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar: {e}. "
247	                "Falling back to grammar_backend='none'. "
248	                "Structured outputs (JSON schema, regex, EBNF) will not be available."
249	            )
250	            server_args.grammar_backend = "none"
251	            return None
252	    elif name == "llguidance":
253	        from sglang.srt.constrained.llguidance_backend import GuidanceBackend
254	
255	        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(
256	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
257	            any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
258	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
259	        )
260	    elif name == "none":
261	        return None
262	    else:
263	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid grammar backend: {name}")
264	
265	    if server_args.reasoning_parser and hasattr(tokenizer, "think_end_id"):
266	        from sglang.srt.constrained.reasoner_grammar_backend import (
267	            ReasonerGrammarBackend,
268	        )
269	
270	        grammar_backend = ReasonerGrammarBackend(
271	            grammar_backend, tokenizer.think_end_id
272	        )
273	
274	    return grammar_backend

155	        # Handle response_format constraints

46	        print("=" * 100)

docs/basic_usage/sampling_params.md

1	# Sampling Parameters
2	
3	This doc describes the sampling parameters of the SGLang Runtime. It is the low-level endpoint of the runtime.
4	If you want a high-level endpoint that can automatically handle chat templates, consider using the [OpenAI Compatible API](openai_api_completions.ipynb).
5	
6	## `/generate` Endpoint
7	
8	The `/generate` endpoint accepts the following parameters in JSON format. For detailed usage, see the [native API doc](native_api.ipynb). The object is defined at `io_struct.py::GenerateReqInput`. You can also read the source code to find more arguments and docs.
9	
10	| Argument                   | Type/Default                                                                 | Description                                                                                                                                                     |
11	|----------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
12	| text                       | `Optional[Union[List[str], str]] = None`                                     | The input prompt. Can be a single prompt or a batch of prompts.                                                                                                 |
13	| input_ids                  | `Optional[Union[List[List[int]], List[int]]] = None`                         | The token IDs for text; one can specify either text or input_ids.                                                                                               |
14	| input_embeds               | `Optional[Union[List[List[List[float]]], List[List[float]]]] = None`         | The embeddings for input_ids; one can specify either text, input_ids, or input_embeds.                                                                          |
15	| image_data                 | `Optional[Union[List[List[ImageDataItem]], List[ImageDataItem], ImageDataItem]] = None` | The image input. Supports three formats: (1) **Raw images**: PIL Image, file path, URL, or base64 string; (2) **Processor output**: Dict with `format: "processor_output"` containing HuggingFace processor outputs; (3) **Precomputed embeddings**: Dict with `format: "precomputed_embedding"` and `feature` containing pre-calculated visual embeddings. Can be a single image, list of images, or list of lists of images. See [Multimodal Input Formats](#multimodal-input-formats) for details. |
16	| audio_data                 | `Optional[Union[List[AudioDataItem], AudioDataItem]] = None`                 | The audio input. Can be a file name, URL, or base64 encoded string.                                                                                             |
17	| sampling_params            | `Optional[Union[List[Dict], Dict]] = None`                                   | The sampling parameters as described in the sections below.                                                                                                     |
18	| rid                        | `Optional[Union[List[str], str]] = None`                                     | The request ID.                                                                                                                                                 |
19	| return_logprob             | `Optional[Union[List[bool], bool]] = None`                                   | Whether to return log probabilities for tokens.                                                                                                                 |
20	| logprob_start_len          | `Optional[Union[List[int], int]] = None`                                     | If return_logprob, the start location in the prompt for returning logprobs. Default is "-1", which returns logprobs for output tokens only.                     |
21	| top_logprobs_num           | `Optional[Union[List[int], int]] = None`                                     | If return_logprob, the number of top logprobs to return at each position.                                                                                       |
22	| token_ids_logprob          | `Optional[Union[List[List[int]], List[int]]] = None`                         | If return_logprob, the token IDs to return logprob for.                                                                                                         |
23	| return_text_in_logprobs    | `bool = False`                                                               | Whether to detokenize tokens in text in the returned logprobs.                                                                                                  |
24	| stream                     | `bool = False`                                                               | Whether to stream output.                                                                                                                                       |
25	| lora_path                  | `Optional[Union[List[Optional[str]], Optional[str]]] = None`                 | The path to the LoRA.                                                                                                                                           |
26	| custom_logit_processor     | `Optional[Union[List[Optional[str]], str]] = None`                           | Custom logit processor for advanced sampling control. Must be a serialized instance of `CustomLogitProcessor` using its `to_str()` method. For usage see below. |
27	| return_hidden_states       | `Union[List[bool], bool] = False`                                            | Whether to return hidden states.                                                                                                                                |
28	| return_routed_experts      | `bool = False`                                                               | Whether to return routed experts for MoE models. Requires `--enable-return-routed-experts` server flag. Returns base64-encoded int32 expert IDs as a flattened array with logical shape `[num_tokens, num_layers, top_k]`. |
29	
30	## Sampling parameters
31	
32	The object is defined at `sampling_params.py::SamplingParams`. You can also read the source code to find more arguments and docs.
33	
34	### Note on defaults
35	
36	By default, SGLang initializes several sampling parameters from the model's `generation_config.json` (when the server is launched with `--sampling-defaults model`, which is the default). To use SGLang/OpenAI constant defaults instead, start the server with `--sampling-defaults openai`. You can always override any parameter per request via `sampling_params`.
37	
38	```bash
39	# Use model-provided defaults from generation_config.json (default behavior)
40	python -m sglang.launch_server --model-path <MODEL> --sampling-defaults model
41	
42	# Use SGLang/OpenAI constant defaults instead
43	python -m sglang.launch_server --model-path <MODEL> --sampling-defaults openai
44	```
45	
46	### Core parameters
47	
48	| Argument        | Type/Default                                 | Description                                                                                                                                    |
49	|-----------------|----------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
50	| max_new_tokens  | `int = 128`                                  | The maximum output length measured in tokens.                                                                                                  |
51	| stop            | `Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None`     | One or multiple [stop words](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create#chat-create-stop). Generation will stop if one of these words is sampled. |
52	| stop_token_ids  | `Optional[List[int]] = None`                 | Provide stop words in the form of token IDs. Generation will stop if one of these token IDs is sampled.                                        |
53	| stop_regex      | `Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None`     | Stop when hitting any of the regex patterns in this list |
54	| temperature     | `float (model default; fallback 1.0)`        | [Temperature](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create#chat-create-temperature) when sampling the next token. `temperature = 0` corresponds to greedy sampling, a higher temperature leads to more diversity. |
55	| top_p           | `float (model default; fallback 1.0)`        | [Top-p](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create#chat-create-top_p) selects tokens from the smallest sorted set whose cumulative probability exceeds `top_p`. When `top_p = 1`, this reduces to unrestricted sampling from all tokens. |
56	| top_k           | `int (model default; fallback -1)`           | [Top-k](https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/how-to-get-better-outputs-from-your-large-language-model/#predictability_vs_creativity) randomly selects from the `k` highest-probability tokens. |
57	| min_p           | `float (model default; fallback 0.0)`        | [Min-p](https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/issues/27670) samples from tokens with probability larger than `min_p * highest_token_probability`. |
58	
59	### Penalizers
60	
61	| Argument           | Type/Default           | Description                                                                                                                                    |
62	|--------------------|------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
63	| frequency_penalty  | `float = 0.0`          | Penalizes tokens based on their frequency in generation so far. Must be between `-2` and `2` where negative numbers encourage repeatment of tokens and positive number encourages sampling of new tokens. The scaling of penalization grows linearly with each appearance of a token. |
64	| presence_penalty   | `float = 0.0`          | Penalizes tokens if they appeared in the generation so far. Must be between `-2` and `2` where negative numbers encourage repeatment of tokens and positive number encourages sampling of new tokens. The scaling of the penalization is constant if a token occurred. |
65	| repetition_penalty | `float = 1.0`          | Scales the logits of previously generated tokens to discourage (values > 1) or encourage (values < 1) repetition. Valid range is `[0, 2]`; `1.0` leaves probabilities unchanged. |
66	| min_new_tokens     | `int = 0`              | Forces the model to generate at least `min_new_tokens` until a stop word or EOS token is sampled. Note that this might lead to unintended behavior, for example, if the distribution is highly skewed towards these tokens. |
67	
68	### Constrained decoding
69	
70	Please refer to our dedicated guide on [constrained decoding](../advanced_features/structured_outputs.ipynb) for the following parameters.
71	
72	| Argument        | Type/Default                    | Description                                                                                                                                    |
73	|-----------------|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
74	| json_schema     | `Optional[str] = None`          | JSON schema for structured outputs.                                                                                                            |
75	| regex           | `Optional[str] = None`          | Regex for structured outputs.                                                                                                                  |
76	| ebnf            | `Optional[str] = None`          | EBNF for structured outputs.                                                                                                                   |
77	| structural_tag  | `Optional[str] = None`          | The structural tag for structured outputs.                                                                                                       |
78	
79	### Other options
80	
81	| Argument                      | Type/Default                    | Description                                                                                                                                    |
82	|-------------------------------|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
83	| n                             | `int = 1`                       | Specifies the number of output sequences to generate per request. (Generating multiple outputs in one request (n > 1) is discouraged; repeating the same prompts several times offers better control and efficiency.) |
84	| ignore_eos                    | `bool = False`                  | Don't stop generation when EOS token is sampled.                                                                                               |
85	| skip_special_tokens           | `bool = True`                   | Remove special tokens during decoding.                                                                                                         |
86	| spaces_between_special_tokens | `bool = True`                   | Whether or not to add spaces between special tokens during detokenization.                                                                     |
87	| no_stop_trim                  | `bool = False`                  | Don't trim stop words or EOS token from the generated text.                                                                                    |
88	| custom_params                 | `Optional[List[Optional[Dict[str, Any]]]] = None` | Used when employing `CustomLogitProcessor`. For usage, see below.                                                                              |
89	
90	## Examples
91	
92	### Normal
93

98	```

196	### Structured Outputs (JSON, Regex, EBNF)
197	
198	You can specify a JSON schema, regular expression or [EBNF](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Extended_Backus%E2%80%93Naur_form) to constrain the model output. The model output will be guaranteed to follow the given constraints. Only one constraint parameter (`json_schema`, `regex`, or `ebnf`) can be specified for a request.
199	
200	SGLang supports two grammar backends:
201	
202	- [XGrammar](https://github.com/mlc-ai/xgrammar) (default): Supports JSON schema, regular expression, and EBNF constraints.
203	  - XGrammar currently uses the [GGML BNF format](https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/master/grammars/README.md).
204	- [Outlines](https://github.com/dottxt-ai/outlines): Supports JSON schema and regular expression constraints.
205	
206	If instead you want to initialize the Outlines backend, you can use `--grammar-backend outlines` flag:
207	
208	```bash
209	python -m sglang.launch_server --model-path meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct \
210	--port 30000 --host 0.0.0.0 --grammar-backend [xgrammar|outlines] # xgrammar or outlines (default: xgrammar)
211	```
212	
213	```python
214	import json
215	import requests
216	
217	json_schema = json.dumps({
218	    "type": "object",
219	    "properties": {
220	        "name": {"type": "string", "pattern": "^[\\w]+$"},
221	        "population": {"type": "integer"},
222	    },
223	    "required": ["name", "population"],
224	})
225	
226	# JSON (works with both Outlines and XGrammar)
227	response = requests.post(
228	    "http://localhost:30000/generate",
229	    json={
230	        "text": "Here is the information of the capital of France in the JSON format.\n",
231	        "sampling_params": {
232	            "temperature": 0,
233	            "max_new_tokens": 64,
234	            "json_schema": json_schema,
235	        },
236	    },
237	)
238	print(response.json())
239	
240	# Regular expression (Outlines backend only)
241	response = requests.post(
242	    "http://localhost:30000/generate",
243	    json={
244	        "text": "Paris is the capital of",
245	        "sampling_params": {
246	            "temperature": 0,
247	            "max_new_tokens": 64,
248	            "regex": "(France|England)",
249	        },
250	    },
251	)
252	print(response.json())
253	
254	# EBNF (XGrammar backend only)
255	response = requests.post(
256	    "http://localhost:30000/generate",
257	    json={
258	        "text": "Write a greeting.",
259	        "sampling_params": {
260	            "temperature": 0,
261	            "max_new_tokens": 64,
262	            "ebnf": 'root ::= "Hello" | "Hi" | "Hey"',
263	        },
264	    },
265	)
266	print(response.json())
267	```
268	
269	Detailed example in [structured outputs](../advanced_features/structured_outputs.ipynb).

271	### Custom logit processor
272	
273	Launch a server with `--enable-custom-logit-processor` flag on.
274	
275	```bash
276	python -m sglang.launch_server \
277	  --model-path meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct \
278	  --port 30000 \
279	  --enable-custom-logit-processor
280	```
281	
282	Define a custom logit processor that will always sample a specific token id.
283	
284	```python
285	from sglang.srt.sampling.custom_logit_processor import CustomLogitProcessor
286	
287	class DeterministicLogitProcessor(CustomLogitProcessor):
288	    """A dummy logit processor that changes the logits to always
289	    sample the given token id.
290	    """
291	
292	    def __call__(self, logits, custom_param_list):
293	        # Check that the number of logits matches the number of custom parameters
294	        assert logits.shape[0] == len(custom_param_list)
295	        key = "token_id"
296	
297	        for i, param_dict in enumerate(custom_param_list):
298	            # Mask all other tokens
299	            logits[i, :] = -float("inf")
300	            # Assign highest probability to the specified token
301	            logits[i, param_dict[key]] = 0.0
302	        return logits
303	```

315	        "sampling_params": {
316	            "temperature": 0.0,
317	            "max_new_tokens": 32,
318	            "custom_params": {"token_id": 5},
319	        },
320	    },
321	)

323	```

347	```

content.text:
I now have a complete picture. Let me write out the detailed plan.

---

## Design Validation and Implementation Plan

### Overall Assessment

Your design is well-structured and the key architectural decision -- converting `json_path` to a JSON schema in `grammar_manager.py` and reusing the existing `dispatch_json()` path -- is the right call. However, I've identified several issues, missing pieces, and refinements after tracing through the actual codebase.

---

### Issue 1: `structural_tag` Is NOT in the Mutual Exclusivity Check (and `json_path` Should Follow the Same Pattern)

Looking at `sampling_params.py` lines 155-161, the existing mutual exclusivity check only covers `json_schema`, `regex`, and `ebnf`. Notably, `structural_tag` is deliberately excluded from this check. This is an inconsistency in the existing code, but it means you have a design choice:

**Recommendation**: Add `json_path` to the mutual exclusivity check alongside `json_schema`, `regex`, and `ebnf`. Also add `structural_tag` to make the check comprehensive. The error message should be updated to include all constraint types. The check should become:

```python
grammars = [
    self.json_schema,
    self.regex,
    self.ebnf,
    self.structural_tag,
    self.json_path,
]
if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
    raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, json_path, ebnf, or structural_tag can be set.")
```

---

### Issue 2: Missing File -- `tokenizer_manager.py` Has a Grammar Detection Method

Your file list misses `python/sglang/srt/managers/tokenizer_manager.py`. At line 1937-1943, the `_request_has_grammar` method explicitly checks for all four constraint types in the sampling params dictionary:

```python
def _request_has_grammar(self, obj: GenerateReqInput) -> bool:
    return (
        obj.sampling_params.get("json_schema", None)
        or obj.sampling_params.get("regex", None)
        or obj.sampling_params.get("ebnf", None)
        or obj.sampling_params.get("structural_tag", None)
    )
```

**You must add `json_path` here**, otherwise requests with `json_path` constraints won't be properly identified as grammar requests. This affects scheduling and routing decisions.

---

### Issue 3: Missing File -- `grpc_server.py`

At `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/grpc_server.py` lines 632-688, the gRPC server explicitly handles each constraint type when building `SamplingParams`. If you want gRPC support (and for completeness), you need to add `json_path` handling there too. If the gRPC proto is external (`smg_grpc_proto`), this may require coordinated changes.

---

### Issue 4: Conversion Placement -- `grammar_manager.py` Is Correct, But There Is a Subtlety

Converting in `grammar_manager.py` is the right place. The key reason is that the `grammar_manager.py` controls cache key creation, and you want caching to work correctly:

- Two requests with the same `json_path` should share the same compiled grammar.
- A `json_path` and a manually-written `json_schema` that happen to produce the same schema should ideally also share cache entries.

For the second point, your conversion should happen BEFORE the cache key lookup. The cache key should be `("json", converted_schema_str)`, not `("json_path", original_path_str)`. This is what your design already specifies, and it's correct -- it means identical schemas from different `json_path` expressions (or from direct `json_schema` input) will share grammar compilations.

However, there's a subtlety: the `process_req_with_grammar()` method at line 67-105 currently checks `req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None` to decide routing. You need to add the `json_path` check and perform the conversion before constructing the cache key. Here's the precise insertion point:

```python
# In process_req_with_grammar(), BEFORE the existing if/elif chain:
if req.sampling_params.json_path is not None:
    from sglang.srt.constrained.json_path_to_schema import json_path_to_schema
    converted_schema = json_path_to_schema(req.sampling_params.json_path)
    req.sampling_params.json_schema = converted_schema
    req.sampling_params.json_path = None  # Clear to avoid confusion
```

Then the existing `json_schema` handling naturally picks it up. The conversion MUST happen before the if/elif chain, not as a separate elif branch.

---

### Issue 5: `SglSamplingParams.clone()` in `ir.py` Does Not Copy `dtype` or `regex`

Looking at `ir.py` lines 42-62, the `clone()` method is positional-only and doesn't include `dtype` or `regex` (they're set as fields but not passed in clone). If you add `json_path` to `SglSamplingParams`, you'll need to decide whether to include it in `clone()`. Given that `json_schema` IS in `clone()` but `regex` and `dtype` are NOT, and `json_path` is semantically similar to `json_schema`, you should include it in `clone()`.

---

### Issue 6: `SglGen` in `ir.py` Uses Positional Arguments

The `SglGen.__init__` and the call site in `api.py` (lines 117-139) pass all parameters positionally. Adding `json_path` means carefully inserting it at the right position or switching to keyword arguments. Looking at the existing pattern, `json_schema` is the last positional arg. You should add `json_path` right after `json_schema` and update ALL call sites (including `gen_int()` and `gen_string()` which pass `None` for the last two positional args).

---

### Issue 7: JSON Path Parsing Edge Cases

The conversion logic you described handles the happy path (`$.store.book[*].author`). Here are edge cases to handle:

1. **Root-only path `$`**: Should produce `{}` (any valid JSON) or `{"type": "object"}`. The empty schema `{}` is more permissive.

2. **Array index `[0]`**: Unlike `[*]`, `[0]` means "first element". This cannot be enforced via JSON schema alone (JSON schema has no concept of "value at index 0"). You should either:
   - Only support `[*]` (wildcard) and reject numeric indices with a clear error.
   - Treat `[0]` the same as `[*]` (array with items matching the nested path) since the schema constrains the structure, not the specific index.

3. **Recursive descent `..`**: Paths like `$.store..price` (find "price" at any depth) are extremely hard to represent as JSON schema. Reject these with a clear error.

4. **Bracket notation `$['store']['book']`**: Equivalent to `$.store.book`. Support both or document which is supported.

5. **Filter expressions `$[?(@.price < 10)]`**: Cannot be represented in JSON schema. Reject with clear error.

6. **Wildcard property `$.*`**: Means any property of root. This can be represented as `{"type": "object"}` with `additionalProperties: {}`.

7. **Multi-property `$['store','warehouse']`**: Means multiple properties. Can be represented with `required` array.

8. **Empty/invalid paths**: Should fail validation with a clear error message.

**Recommendation**: For the initial implementation, support only the dot-notation subset:
- `$` (root)
- `.property` (property access)
- `[*]` (array wildcard)

Reject everything else (`..`, `[0]`, `[?(...)]`, bracket notation) with an explicit `ValueError` listing what's supported.

---

### Issue 8: The Frontend Language Layer (`api.py`, `ir.py`, `interpreter.py`) -- Is It Necessary?

Looking at the codebase, `structural_tag` does NOT exist in the frontend language layer (`api.py`, `ir.py`, `interpreter.py`). It was added only at the SRT backend level. The frontend layer only has `json_schema`, `regex`, and `dtype`.

You have two options:
- **Option A**: Follow `structural_tag`'s pattern and only add `json_path` at the SRT level (files 1-6 in your list). Skip files 7-10.
- **Option B**: Add to both layers as you planned.

**Recommendation**: Go with Option B (your plan) since `json_path` is a user-facing feature that benefits from first-class frontend support. Users of the `sgl.gen()` API should be able to write `sgl.gen("output", json_path="$.store.book[*].author")`.

---

### Issue 9: `has_existing_constraints` Check in `ChatCompletionRequest.to_sampling_params()`

At `protocol.py` line 762-767, there's a check for existing constraints that prevents tool call constraints from overriding user-specified constraints:

```python
has_existing_constraints = (
    sampling_params.get("regex")
    or sampling_params.get("ebnf")
    or sampling_params.get("structural_tag")
    or sampling_params.get("json_schema")
)
```

You must add `json_path` here too. Similarly, `_build_sampling_params` in `serving_completions.py` needs `json_path` added to the dict.

---

### Revised Complete File List

**New files:**
1. `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path_to_schema.py` -- Conversion utility

**Modified files (SRT backend layer):**
2. `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` -- Add `json_path` parameter, add to mutual exclusivity
3. `python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` -- Convert `json_path` to `json_schema` before cache key, add to grammar detection condition
4. `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` -- Add `json_path` to `CompletionRequest`, `ChatCompletionRequest`, `has_existing_constraints` check, and `to_sampling_params()`
5. `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_completions.py` -- Add `json_path` to `_build_sampling_params()` dict
6. `python/sglang/srt/managers/tokenizer_manager.py` -- Add `json_path` to `_request_has_grammar()`

**Modified files (Frontend language layer):**
8. `python/sglang/lang/api.py` -- Add `json_path` to `gen()` function signature and `SglGen` call
9. `python/sglang/lang/ir.py` -- Add `json_path` to `SglSamplingParams`, `clone()`, `to_srt_kwargs()`, and `SglGen.__init__()`
10. `python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py` -- Add `json_path` to the `_resolve_sampling_params()` field list (line 807-809)
11. `python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py` -- `json_path` flows naturally through `to_srt_kwargs()` once `ir.py` is updated, no separate change needed

**Documentation:**
12. `docs/basic_usage/sampling_params.md` -- Add `json_path` to the constrained decoding table

**Tests:**
13. New test file or new mixin: unit tests for `json_path_to_schema.py` conversion
14. New test kit: `python/sglang/test/kits/json_path_constrained_kit.py` -- Integration test following the pattern of `json_constrained_kit.py`

---

### Step-by-Step Implementation Sequence

**Phase 1: Core conversion utility (no dependencies)**
1. Create `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path_to_schema.py`
   - Implement `json_path_to_schema(path: str) -> str` that returns a JSON schema string
   - Parse path segments: `$`, `.property`, `[*]`
   - Reject unsupported patterns (recursive descent `..`, numeric indices `[0]`, filter expressions `[?()]`)
   - Validate input: must start with `$`, must not be empty
   - Write pure unit tests for this module (no server needed)

**Phase 2: SRT backend plumbing (bottom-up)**
2. Modify `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`
   - Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` parameter to `__init__`
   - Store `self.json_path = json_path`
   - Add `self.json_path` to the `grammars` list in `verify()`
   - Update error message

3. Modify `python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`
   - Add `json_path` to the condition check at line 71-75
   - Add conversion logic BEFORE the if/elif chain for cache key creation
   - Specifically: if `json_path` is set, convert to schema, set as `json_schema`, clear `json_path`

4. Modify `python/sglang/srt/managers/tokenizer_manager.py`
   - Add `obj.sampling_params.get("json_path", None)` to `_request_has_grammar()`

**Phase 3: API layer (top-down)**
5. Modify `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`
   - Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` to `CompletionRequest` (after `ebnf` field, line ~270)
   - Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` to `ChatCompletionRequest` (after `ebnf` field, line ~581)
   - Add `json_path` to `has_existing_constraints` check in `to_sampling_params()` (line ~762)
   - Add `"json_path": self.json_path` to the sampling_params dict in `to_sampling_params()` (in the initial dict, not the response_format handling)

6. Modify `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_completions.py`
   - Add `"json_path": request.json_path` to the `_build_sampling_params()` dict

**Phase 4: Frontend language layer**
7. Modify `python/sglang/lang/ir.py`
   - Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` to `SglSamplingParams` dataclass
   - Add `json_path` to `clone()` method
   - Add `"json_path": self.json_path` to `to_srt_kwargs()` return dict
   - Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` to `SglGen.__init__()` signature
   - Pass `json_path=json_path` to `SglSamplingParams` in `SglGen.__init__()`

8. Modify `python/sglang/lang/api.py`
   - Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` to `gen()` function signature
   - Pass `json_path` to the `SglGen` constructor call
   - Update `gen_int()` and `gen_string()` to pass `None` for the new positional arg (or better: convert to keyword args in the `SglGen` call)

9. Modify `python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py`
   - Add `"json_path"` to the field list at line ~807-809 in `_resolve_sampling_params()`

**Phase 5: Documentation and tests**
10. Update `docs/basic_usage/sampling_params.md` -- Add `json_path` row to constrained decoding table
11. Write unit tests for `json_path_to_schema.py` (pure Python, no server)
12. Write integration test kit `json_path_constrained_kit.py`
13. Add the mixin to `test_constrained_decoding.py`

---

### Testing Strategy

**Unit tests** (for `json_path_to_schema.py`):
- Simple path: `$.name` -> schema with required "name" property
- Nested path: `$.store.book` -> nested objects with required properties
- Array wildcard: `$.items[*].name` -> array with items containing required "name"
- Deep nesting: `$.a.b.c.d[*].e` -> deeply nested schema
- Root only: `$` -> minimal schema
- Invalid inputs: empty string, missing `$`, `..`, `[0]`, filter expressions -> `ValueError`
- Malformed paths: `$.`, `$..`, `$[`, `$.store.` -> `ValueError`

**Integration tests** (require running server):
- Send a `/generate` request with `json_path` in sampling params, verify output is valid JSON matching the path structure
- Send an OpenAI-compatible chat completion with `json_path`, verify structured output
- Test mutual exclusivity: sending both `json_path` and `json_schema` should return an error
- Test `sgl.gen()` with `json_path` parameter (frontend language test)
- Test caching: two requests with the same `json_path` should be fast on the second request

---

### Potential Challenges

1. **Schema permissiveness**: The generated schema only constrains structure (required properties, array types), not leaf value types. A path like `$.store.book[*].author` will require `author` to exist but allow any type for its value. This is intentionally permissive -- the LLM will naturally produce strings for "author", and the schema ensures the structural constraint.

2. **`additionalProperties`**: The generated schema should NOT set `additionalProperties: false` by default. This allows the LLM to add extra fields beyond what the path requires, which is more natural for generation. The path constraint means "this path must exist", not "only this path exists".

3. **Performance**: The `json_path_to_schema` conversion is string parsing and dict construction -- it's trivially fast (microseconds). The real cost is in grammar compilation, which is the same regardless of whether the schema came from `json_path` or direct `json_schema`. No performance concern.

4. **SglGen positional arguments**: The biggest mechanical risk is the positional argument threading in `api.py` -> `SglGen` -> `SglSamplingParams`. Consider converting the `SglGen` constructor call in `api.py` to use keyword arguments to avoid ordering bugs. This is a defensive change.

- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504005815-rjdob3/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`

I need to design an implementation plan for adding a `json_path` output constraint to SGLang. Here's the full context:

## Goal
Add a new "json_path" constraint that ensures generated output is valid JSON containing a specific JSON path pattern (e.g., `"$.store.book[*].author"`). It should be registered alongside existing constraints (`json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, `structural_tag`).

## Existing Architecture

### Constraint Flow (top to bottom):
1. **User API** (`python/sglang/lang/api.py`): `gen()` function accepts `regex`, `json_schema`, `dtype`, `choices` 
2. **IR** (`python/sglang/lang/ir.py`): `SglSamplingParams` dataclass stores `json_schema`, `regex`, `dtype`; `SglGen` creates these
3. **Interpreter** (`python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py`): `_resolve_sampling_params()` merges default + override params (line 807-809 handles `dtype`, `regex`, `json_schema`)
4. **Backend** (`python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py`): `_handle_dtype_to_regex()` converts `dtype` to `regex` before sending to SRT. `to_srt_kwargs()` serializes params.
5. **SRT SamplingParams** (`python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`): `SamplingParams.__init__()` stores `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, `structural_tag`. `verify()` enforces mutual exclusivity.
6. **OpenAI Protocol** (`python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`): `CompletionRequest` (line 268-270) and `ChatCompletionRequest` (line 580-581) have `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf` fields
7. **Serving** (`python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_completions.py`): `_build_sampling_params()` passes `regex`, `json_schema`, `ebnf` to SamplingParams
8. **Grammar Manager** (`python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`): `process_req_with_grammar()` routes based on constraint type, creates cache key like `("json", schema_str)`, `("regex", regex_str)`, etc.
9. **Grammar Backends** (`base_grammar_backend.py`): `_init_value_dispatch()` dispatches to `dispatch_json()`, `dispatch_regex()`, `dispatch_ebnf()`, `dispatch_structural_tag()`. Each backend (xgrammar, outlines, llguidance) implements these.

### Key Design Decisions I've Made:
- **`json_path` will be converted to a JSON schema** and then dispatched through the existing `dispatch_json()` path. This avoids changing any grammar backend.
- **The conversion happens in `grammar_manager.py`**: `json_path` flows as a first-class parameter through the entire stack, but at the grammar manager level, it's converted to a JSON schema and dispatched as `("json", converted_schema_str)`.
- A new utility module `json_path_to_schema.py` handles the conversion.

### JSON Path → JSON Schema Conversion Logic:
For a path like `"$.store.book[*].author"`:
- `$` → root object `{"type": "object"}`
- `.store` → requires "store" property `{"required": ["store"], "properties": {"store": {...}}}`
- `.book` → requires "book" property
- `[*]` → array with items `{"type": "array", "items": {...}}`
- `.author` → requires "author" property

Result schema:
```json
{
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["store"],
  "properties": {
    "store": {
      "type": "object", 
      "required": ["book"],
      "properties": {
        "book": {
          "type": "array",
          "items": {
            "type": "object",
            "required": ["author"],
            "properties": {
              "author": {}
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Files to Modify:
1. **NEW: `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path_to_schema.py`** - Conversion utility
2. **`python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`** - Add `json_path` parameter + mutual exclusivity
3. **`python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`** - Handle `json_path` routing → convert to json_schema dispatch
4. **`python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`** - Add `json_path` to CompletionRequest + ChatCompletionRequest
5. **`python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_completions.py`** - Include `json_path` in build params
6. **`python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_chat.py`** - Include `json_path` in to_sampling_params()
7. **`python/sglang/lang/api.py`** - Add `json_path` param to `gen()`
8. **`python/sglang/lang/ir.py`** - Add to `SglSamplingParams`, `SglGen`, `to_srt_kwargs()`
9. **`python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py`** - Add `json_path` to resolved params
10. **`python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py`** - Pass `json_path` through `to_srt_kwargs()`

Please validate this design and identify any issues, missing pieces, or improvements. Consider:
- Should `json_path` be in the mutual exclusivity check with `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`?
- Are there any edge cases in the JSON path parsing I should handle?
- Is the grammar_manager the right place for conversion, or should it be elsewhere?
- Any testing strategy considerations?

content.text:
I now have a complete picture. Let me write out the detailed plan.

---

## Design Validation and Implementation Plan

### Overall Assessment

Your design is well-structured and the key architectural decision -- converting `json_path` to a JSON schema in `grammar_manager.py` and reusing the existing `dispatch_json()` path -- is the right call. However, I've identified several issues, missing pieces, and refinements after tracing through the actual codebase.

---

### Issue 1: `structural_tag` Is NOT in the Mutual Exclusivity Check (and `json_path` Should Follow the Same Pattern)

Looking at `sampling_params.py` lines 155-161, the existing mutual exclusivity check only covers `json_schema`, `regex`, and `ebnf`. Notably, `structural_tag` is deliberately excluded from this check. This is an inconsistency in the existing code, but it means you have a design choice:

**Recommendation**: Add `json_path` to the mutual exclusivity check alongside `json_schema`, `regex`, and `ebnf`. Also add `structural_tag` to make the check comprehensive. The error message should be updated to include all constraint types. The check should become:

```python
grammars = [
    self.json_schema,
    self.regex,
    self.ebnf,
    self.structural_tag,
    self.json_path,
]
if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
    raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, json_path, ebnf, or structural_tag can be set.")
```

---

### Issue 2: Missing File -- `tokenizer_manager.py` Has a Grammar Detection Method

Your file list misses `python/sglang/srt/managers/tokenizer_manager.py`. At line 1937-1943, the `_request_has_grammar` method explicitly checks for all four constraint types in the sampling params dictionary:

```python
def _request_has_grammar(self, obj: GenerateReqInput) -> bool:
    return (
        obj.sampling_params.get("json_schema", None)
        or obj.sampling_params.get("regex", None)
        or obj.sampling_params.get("ebnf", None)
        or obj.sampling_params.get("structural_tag", None)
    )
```

**You must add `json_path` here**, otherwise requests with `json_path` constraints won't be properly identified as grammar requests. This affects scheduling and routing decisions.

---

### Issue 3: Missing File -- `grpc_server.py`

At `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/grpc_server.py` lines 632-688, the gRPC server explicitly handles each constraint type when building `SamplingParams`. If you want gRPC support (and for completeness), you need to add `json_path` handling there too. If the gRPC proto is external (`smg_grpc_proto`), this may require coordinated changes.

---

### Issue 4: Conversion Placement -- `grammar_manager.py` Is Correct, But There Is a Subtlety

Converting in `grammar_manager.py` is the right place. The key reason is that the `grammar_manager.py` controls cache key creation, and you want caching to work correctly:

- Two requests with the same `json_path` should share the same compiled grammar.
- A `json_path` and a manually-written `json_schema` that happen to produce the same schema should ideally also share cache entries.

For the second point, your conversion should happen BEFORE the cache key lookup. The cache key should be `("json", converted_schema_str)`, not `("json_path", original_path_str)`. This is what your design already specifies, and it's correct -- it means identical schemas from different `json_path` expressions (or from direct `json_schema` input) will share grammar compilations.

However, there's a subtlety: the `process_req_with_grammar()` method at line 67-105 currently checks `req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None` to decide routing. You need to add the `json_path` check and perform the conversion before constructing the cache key. Here's the precise insertion point:

```python
# In process_req_with_grammar(), BEFORE the existing if/elif chain:
if req.sampling_params.json_path is not None:
    from sglang.srt.constrained.json_path_to_schema import json_path_to_schema
    converted_schema = json_path_to_schema(req.sampling_params.json_path)
    req.sampling_params.json_schema = converted_schema
    req.sampling_params.json_path = None  # Clear to avoid confusion
```

Then the existing `json_schema` handling naturally picks it up. The conversion MUST happen before the if/elif chain, not as a separate elif branch.

---

### Issue 5: `SglSamplingParams.clone()` in `ir.py` Does Not Copy `dtype` or `regex`

Looking at `ir.py` lines 42-62, the `clone()` method is positional-only and doesn't include `dtype` or `regex` (they're set as fields but not passed in clone). If you add `json_path` to `SglSamplingParams`, you'll need to decide whether to include it in `clone()`. Given that `json_schema` IS in `clone()` but `regex` and `dtype` are NOT, and `json_path` is semantically similar to `json_schema`, you should include it in `clone()`.

---

### Issue 6: `SglGen` in `ir.py` Uses Positional Arguments

The `SglGen.__init__` and the call site in `api.py` (lines 117-139) pass all parameters positionally. Adding `json_path` means carefully inserting it at the right position or switching to keyword arguments. Looking at the existing pattern, `json_schema` is the last positional arg. You should add `json_path` right after `json_schema` and update ALL call sites (including `gen_int()` and `gen_string()` which pass `None` for the last two positional args).

---

### Issue 7: JSON Path Parsing Edge Cases

The conversion logic you described handles the happy path (`$.store.book[*].author`). Here are edge cases to handle:

1. **Root-only path `$`**: Should produce `{}` (any valid JSON) or `{"type": "object"}`. The empty schema `{}` is more permissive.

2. **Array index `[0]`**: Unlike `[*]`, `[0]` means "first element". This cannot be enforced via JSON schema alone (JSON schema has no concept of "value at index 0"). You should either:
   - Only support `[*]` (wildcard) and reject numeric indices with a clear error.
   - Treat `[0]` the same as `[*]` (array with items matching the nested path) since the schema constrains the structure, not the specific index.

3. **Recursive descent `..`**: Paths like `$.store..price` (find "price" at any depth) are extremely hard to represent as JSON schema. Reject these with a clear error.

4. **Bracket notation `$['store']['book']`**: Equivalent to `$.store.book`. Support both or document which is supported.

5. **Filter expressions `$[?(@.price < 10)]`**: Cannot be represented in JSON schema. Reject with clear error.

6. **Wildcard property `$.*`**: Means any property of root. This can be represented as `{"type": "object"}` with `additionalProperties: {}`.

7. **Multi-property `$['store','warehouse']`**: Means multiple properties. Can be represented with `required` array.

8. **Empty/invalid paths**: Should fail validation with a clear error message.

**Recommendation**: For the initial implementation, support only the dot-notation subset:
- `$` (root)
- `.property` (property access)
- `[*]` (array wildcard)

Reject everything else (`..`, `[0]`, `[?(...)]`, bracket notation) with an explicit `ValueError` listing what's supported.

---

### Issue 8: The Frontend Language Layer (`api.py`, `ir.py`, `interpreter.py`) -- Is It Necessary?

Looking at the codebase, `structural_tag` does NOT exist in the frontend language layer (`api.py`, `ir.py`, `interpreter.py`). It was added only at the SRT backend level. The frontend layer only has `json_schema`, `regex`, and `dtype`.

You have two options:
- **Option A**: Follow `structural_tag`'s pattern and only add `json_path` at the SRT level (files 1-6 in your list). Skip files 7-10.
- **Option B**: Add to both layers as you planned.

**Recommendation**: Go with Option B (your plan) since `json_path` is a user-facing feature that benefits from first-class frontend support. Users of the `sgl.gen()` API should be able to write `sgl.gen("output", json_path="$.store.book[*].author")`.

---

### Issue 9: `has_existing_constraints` Check in `ChatCompletionRequest.to_sampling_params()`

At `protocol.py` line 762-767, there's a check for existing constraints that prevents tool call constraints from overriding user-specified constraints:

```python
has_existing_constraints = (
    sampling_params.get("regex")
    or sampling_params.get("ebnf")
    or sampling_params.get("structural_tag")
    or sampling_params.get("json_schema")
)
```

You must add `json_path` here too. Similarly, `_build_sampling_params` in `serving_completions.py` needs `json_path` added to the dict.

---

### Revised Complete File List

**New files:**
1. `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path_to_schema.py` -- Conversion utility

**Modified files (SRT backend layer):**
2. `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` -- Add `json_path` parameter, add to mutual exclusivity
3. `python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` -- Convert `json_path` to `json_schema` before cache key, add to grammar detection condition

5. `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_completions.py` -- Add `json_path` to `_build_sampling_params()` dict
6. `python/sglang/srt/managers/tokenizer_manager.py` -- Add `json_path` to `_request_has_grammar()`
7. `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/grpc_server.py` -- Add `json_path` handling (optional, depends on proto availability)

**Modified files (Frontend language layer):**
8. `python/sglang/lang/api.py` -- Add `json_path` to `gen()` function signature and `SglGen` call
9. `python/sglang/lang/ir.py` -- Add `json_path` to `SglSamplingParams`, `clone()`, `to_srt_kwargs()`, and `SglGen.__init__()`
10. `python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py` -- Add `json_path` to the `_resolve_sampling_params()` field list (line 807-809)
11. `python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py` -- `json_path` flows naturally through `to_srt_kwargs()` once `ir.py` is updated, no separate change needed

**Documentation:**
12. `docs/basic_usage/sampling_params.md` -- Add `json_path` to the constrained decoding table

**Tests:**
13. New test file or new mixin: unit tests for `json_path_to_schema.py` conversion
14. New test kit: `python/sglang/test/kits/json_path_constrained_kit.py` -- Integration test following the pattern of `json_constrained_kit.py`

---

### Step-by-Step Implementation Sequence

**Phase 1: Core conversion utility (no dependencies)**
1. Create `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path_to_schema.py`
   - Implement `json_path_to_schema(path: str) -> str` that returns a JSON schema string

3. Modify `python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`

   - Specifically: if `json_path` is set, convert to schema, set as `json_schema`, clear `json_path`

4. Modify `python/sglang/srt/managers/tokenizer_manager.py`

**Phase 3: API layer (top-down)**
5. Modify `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`
   - Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` to `CompletionRequest` (after `ebnf` field, line ~270)
   - Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` to `ChatCompletionRequest` (after `ebnf` field, line ~581)
   - Add `json_path` to `has_existing_constraints` check in `to_sampling_params()` (line ~762)

6. Modify `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_completions.py`
   - Add `"json_path": request.json_path` to the `_build_sampling_params()` dict

**Phase 4: Frontend language layer**
7. Modify `python/sglang/lang/ir.py`
   - Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` to `SglSamplingParams` dataclass
   - Add `json_path` to `clone()` method
   - Add `"json_path": self.json_path` to `to_srt_kwargs()` return dict
   - Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` to `SglGen.__init__()` signature
   - Pass `json_path=json_path` to `SglSamplingParams` in `SglGen.__init__()`

8. Modify `python/sglang/lang/api.py`
   - Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` to `gen()` function signature
   - Pass `json_path` to the `SglGen` constructor call
   - Update `gen_int()` and `gen_string()` to pass `None` for the new positional arg (or better: convert to keyword args in the `SglGen` call)

9. Modify `python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py`
   - Add `"json_path"` to the field list at line ~807-809 in `_resolve_sampling_params()`

**Phase 5: Documentation and tests**
10. Update `docs/basic_usage/sampling_params.md` -- Add `json_path` row to constrained decoding table
11. Write unit tests for `json_path_to_schema.py` (pure Python, no server)
12. Write integration test kit `json_path_constrained_kit.py`
13. Add the mixin to `test_constrained_decoding.py`

---

### Testing Strategy

**Unit tests** (for `json_path_to_schema.py`):
- Simple path: `$.name` -> schema with required "name" property
- Nested path: `$.store.book` -> nested objects with required properties
- Array wildcard: `$.items[*].name` -> array with items containing required "name"
- Deep nesting: `$.a.b.c.d[*].e` -> deeply nested schema
- Root only: `$` -> minimal schema
- Invalid inputs: empty string, missing `$`, `..`, `[0]`, filter expressions -> `ValueError`
- Malformed paths: `$.`, `$..`, `$[`, `$.store.` -> `ValueError`

**Integration tests** (require running server):
- Send a `/generate` request with `json_path` in sampling params, verify output is valid JSON matching the path structure
- Send an OpenAI-compatible chat completion with `json_path`, verify structured output
- Test mutual exclusivity: sending both `json_path` and `json_schema` should return an error
- Test `sgl.gen()` with `json_path` parameter (frontend language test)
- Test caching: two requests with the same `json_path` should be fast on the second request

---

### Potential Challenges

1. **Schema permissiveness**: The generated schema only constrains structure (required properties, array types), not leaf value types. A path like `$.store.book[*].author` will require `author` to exist but allow any type for its value. This is intentionally permissive -- the LLM will naturally produce strings for "author", and the schema ensures the structural constraint.

2. **`additionalProperties`**: The generated schema should NOT set `additionalProperties: false` by default. This allows the LLM to add extra fields beyond what the path requires, which is more natural for generation. The path constraint means "this path must exist", not "only this path exists".

3. **Performance**: The `json_path_to_schema` conversion is string parsing and dict construction -- it's trivially fast (microseconds). The real cost is in grammar compilation, which is the same regardless of whether the schema came from `json_path` or direct `json_schema`. No performance concern.

4. **SglGen positional arguments**: The biggest mechanical risk is the positional argument threading in `api.py` -> `SglGen` -> `SglSamplingParams`. Consider converting the `SglGen` constructor call in `api.py` to use keyword arguments to avoid ordering bugs. This is a defensive change.

44	```

47

57	| min_p           | `float (model default; fallback 0.0)`        | [Min-p](https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/issues/27670) samples from tokens with probability larger than `min_p * highest_token_probability`. |

62	|--------------------|------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
63	| frequency_penalty  | `float = 0.0`          | Penalizes tokens based on their frequency in generation so far. Must be between `-2` and `2` where negative numbers encourage repeatment of tokens and positive number encourages sampling of new tokens. The scaling of penalization grows linearly with each appearance of a token. |
64	| presence_penalty   | `float = 0.0`          | Penalizes tokens if they appeared in the generation so far. Must be between `-2` and `2` where negative numbers encourage repeatment of tokens and positive number encourages sampling of new tokens. The scaling of the penalization is constant if a token occurred. |
65	| repetition_penalty | `float = 1.0`          | Scales the logits of previously generated tokens to discourage (values > 1) or encourage (values < 1) repetition. Valid range is `[0, 2]`; `1.0` leaves probabilities unchanged. |
66	| min_new_tokens     | `int = 0`              | Forces the model to generate at least `min_new_tokens` until a stop word or EOS token is sampled. Note that this might lead to unintended behavior, for example, if the distribution is highly skewed towards these tokens. |
