14	"""Constrained decoding with xgrammar backend."""
15	
16	import dataclasses
17	import json
18	import logging
19	from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple, Union
20	
21	import torch
22	from xgrammar import (
23	    CompiledGrammar,
24	    GrammarCompiler,
25	    GrammarMatcher,
26	    StructuralTagItem,
27	    TokenizerInfo,
28	    allocate_token_bitmask,
29	)
30	
31	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
32	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
33	    BaseGrammarBackend,
34	    BaseGrammarObject,
35	    GrammarStats,
36	)
37	from sglang.srt.constrained.utils import is_legacy_structural_tag
38	from sglang.srt.utils import is_hip
39	
40	_is_hip = is_hip()
41	if _is_hip:
42	    from sgl_kernel import apply_token_bitmask_inplace_cuda
43	else:
44	    from sglang.srt.constrained.triton_ops.bitmask_ops import (
45	        apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton,
46	    )
47	
48	
49	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
50	MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS = 200
51	
52	
53	class XGrammarGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
54	
55	    def __init__(
56	        self,
57	        matcher: GrammarMatcher,
58	        vocab_size: int,
59	        ctx: CompiledGrammar,
60	        override_stop_tokens: Optional[Union[List[int], int]],
61	        key_string: Optional[str] = None,  # TODO (sk): for debugging, remove later
62	        grammar_stats: Optional[GrammarStats] = GrammarStats(),
63	    ) -> None:
64	        super().__init__()
65	        self.matcher = matcher
66	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
67	        self.ctx = ctx
68	        self.override_stop_tokens = override_stop_tokens
69	        self.accepted_tokens = []
70	        self.key_string = key_string
71	        self.grammar_stats = grammar_stats
72	
73	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
74	        if not self.is_terminated():
75	            self.current_token = token
76	            accepted = self.matcher.accept_token(token)
77	            if not accepted:
78	                # log for debugging
79	                raise ValueError(
80	                    f"Tokens not accepted: {token}\n"
81	                    f"Accepted tokens: {self.accepted_tokens}\n"
82	                    f"Key string: {self.key_string}"
83	                )
84	            else:
85	                self.accepted_tokens.append(token)
86	
87	    def rollback(self, k: int):
88	        self.matcher.rollback(k)
89	        self.accepted_tokens = self.accepted_tokens[:-k]
90	
91	    def is_terminated(self):
92	        return self.matcher.is_terminated()
93	
94	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
95	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
96	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
97	        return allocate_token_bitmask(batch_size, vocab_size)
98	
99	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
100	        self.matcher.fill_next_token_bitmask(vocab_mask, idx)
101	
102	    @staticmethod
103	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
104	        return vocab_mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
105	
106	    def apply_vocab_mask(self, logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
107	        if (
108	            logits.device.type == "cuda"
109	            or logits.device.type == "npu"
110	            or logits.device.type == "xpu"
111	        ):
112	            if _is_hip:
113	                apply_token_bitmask_inplace_cuda(logits, vocab_mask)
114	            else:
115	                apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton(logits, vocab_mask)
116	        elif logits.device.type == "cpu" and self.apply_vocab_mask_cpu:
117	            self.apply_vocab_mask_cpu(logits, vocab_mask)
118	        else:
119	            raise RuntimeError(f"Unsupported device: {logits.device.type}")
120	
121	    def copy(self):
122	        matcher = GrammarMatcher(
123	            self.ctx,
124	            max_rollback_tokens=MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS,
125	            override_stop_tokens=self.override_stop_tokens,
126	        )
127	        return XGrammarGrammar(
128	            matcher,
129	            self.vocab_size,
130	            self.ctx,
131	            self.override_stop_tokens,
132	            self.key_string,
133	            dataclasses.replace(
134	                self.grammar_stats, is_cache_hit=True, tree_traversal_time=[]
135	            ),
136	        )
137	
138	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
139	        s = self.matcher.find_jump_forward_string()
140	        if s:
141	            return [], s
142	        return None
143	
144	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
145	        _, data = helper
146	        return data, -1
147	
148	    def jump_and_retokenize(
149	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
150	    ):
151	        k = 0
152	        for i, old_id in enumerate(old_output_ids):
153	            if old_id == new_output_ids[i]:
154	                k = i + 1
155	            else:
156	                break
157	
158	        # rollback to the last token that is the same
159	        if k < len(old_output_ids):
160	            self.matcher.rollback(len(old_output_ids) - k)
161	
162	        for i in range(k, len(new_output_ids)):
163	            assert self.matcher.accept_token(new_output_ids[i])
164	
165	    def __repr__(self):
166	        return f"XGrammarGrammar({self.key_string=}, {self.accepted_tokens=}, {self.current_token=})"
167	
168	
169	class TokenizerNotSupportedError(Exception):
170	    """Raised when tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar backend."""
171	
172	    pass
173	
174	
175	class XGrammarGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
176	    def __init__(
177	        self,
178	        tokenizer,
179	        vocab_size: int,
180	        model_eos_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
181	        any_whitespace: bool = True,
182	    ):
183	        super().__init__()
184	
185	        if hasattr(tokenizer, "init_xgrammar"):
186	            # For special tokenizer
187	            tokenizer_info, override_stop_tokens = tokenizer.init_xgrammar()
188	
189	            if tokenizer_info is None:
190	                # Not supported tokenizer
191	                raise TokenizerNotSupportedError(
192	                    f"Tokenizer type {type(tokenizer).__name__} is not supported by XGrammar"
193	                )
194	        else:
195	            # Create TokenizerInfo with model's EOS tokens as the authoritative stop tokens
196	            # This ensures consistency between what the model considers EOS and what XGrammar uses
197	            try:
198	                tokenizer_info = TokenizerInfo.from_huggingface(
199	                    tokenizer, vocab_size=vocab_size, stop_token_ids=model_eos_token_ids
200	                )
201	                override_stop_tokens = None
202	            except Exception as e:
203	                raise TokenizerNotSupportedError(
204	                    f"Failed to create XGrammar TokenizerInfo from tokenizer: {e}"
205	                )
206	
207	        self.grammar_compiler = GrammarCompiler(tokenizer_info=tokenizer_info)
208	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
209	        self.override_stop_tokens = override_stop_tokens
210	        self.any_whitespace = any_whitespace
211	
212	    @staticmethod
213	    def _sanitize_structural_format(structural_format):
214	        """Recursively replace missing json_schema fields with an empty schema."""
215	        if not isinstance(structural_format, dict):
216	            return
217	
218	        fmt_type = structural_format.get("type")
219	        if fmt_type in {"json_schema", "qwen_xml_parameter"}:
220	            if structural_format.get("json_schema") is None:
221	                structural_format["json_schema"] = {}
222	
223	        if fmt_type == "tag":
224	            XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(
225	                structural_format.get("content")
226	            )
227	        elif fmt_type in {"sequence", "or"}:
228	            for element in structural_format.get("elements", []):
229	                XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(element)
230	        elif fmt_type in {"triggered_tags", "tags_with_separator"}:
231	            for tag in structural_format.get("tags", []):
232	                XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(tag)
233	
234	    @staticmethod
235	    def _sanitize_structural_tag_structures(structural_tag: Dict) -> None:
236	        for structure in structural_tag.get("structures", []):
237	            if structure.get("schema") is None:
238	                structure["schema"] = {}
239	
240	    def _from_context(
241	        self, ctx: CompiledGrammar, key_string: str, grammar_stats: GrammarStats
242	    ) -> XGrammarGrammar:
243	        matcher = GrammarMatcher(
244	            ctx,
245	            max_rollback_tokens=MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS,
246	            override_stop_tokens=self.override_stop_tokens,
247	        )
248	        return XGrammarGrammar(
249	            matcher,
250	            self.vocab_size,
251	            ctx,
252	            self.override_stop_tokens,
253	            key_string,
254	            grammar_stats,
255	        )
256	
257	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
258	        try:
259	            if key_string == "$$ANY$$":
260	                # Note: This builtin JSON grammar includes *all* valid JSON (including, for example, arrays at the root)
261	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_builtin_json_grammar()
262	            else:
263	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(
264	                    schema=key_string, any_whitespace=self.any_whitespace
265	                )
266	
267	        except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError) as e:
268	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
269	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
270	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json"))
271	
272	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
273	        try:
274	            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_grammar(key_string)
275	        except RuntimeError as e:
276	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid ebnf: {key_string=}, {e=}")
277	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
278	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="ebnf"))
279	
280	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
281	        try:
282	            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_regex(key_string)
283	        except RuntimeError as e:
284	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex: {key_string=}, {e=}")
285	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
286	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="regex"))
287	
288	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
289	        try:
290	            # TODO(dark): it's REALLY stupid to construct object from string and decode it again
291	            structural_tag = json.loads(key_string)
292	            if is_legacy_structural_tag(structural_tag):
293	                self._sanitize_structural_tag_structures(structural_tag)
294	                tags = [
295	                    StructuralTagItem(
296	                        begin=structure["begin"],
297	                        schema=json.dumps(structure["schema"]),
298	                        end=structure["end"],
299	                    )
300	                    for structure in structural_tag["structures"]
301	                ]

305	            else:

31	class SamplingParams:
32	    """
33	    The sampling parameters.
34	
35	    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or

40	    def __init__(
41	        self,
42	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
43	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
44	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
45	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
46	        temperature: float = 1.0,
47	        top_p: float = 1.0,
48	        top_k: int = -1,
49	        min_p: float = 0.0,

51	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
52	        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
53	        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
54	        n: int = 1,
55	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
56	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
57	        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
58	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
61	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
65	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
66	        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
67	    ) -> None:
68	        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
69	        self.stop_strs = stop
70	        if stop_token_ids:
71	            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
91	        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
92	        self.custom_params = custom_params
93	        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
94	        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
95	        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed
96	
97	        # Process some special cases
98	        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
99	            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
100	            self.temperature = 1.0
101	            self.top_k = 1
102	        if self.top_k == -1:
103	            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary
104	
105	    def verify(self, vocab_size):
106	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
107	            raise ValueError(
108	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
109	            )
110	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
111	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
112	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
113	            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
114	        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
115	            raise ValueError(
116	                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
117	            )
118	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
119	            raise ValueError(
120	                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
121	                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
122	            )
123	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
124	            raise ValueError(
125	                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
126	            )
127	        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
128	            raise ValueError(
129	                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
130	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
131	            )
132	        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
133	            raise ValueError(
134	                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
135	                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
136	            )
137	        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
138	            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
139	                raise ValueError(
140	                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
141	                )
142	            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
143	                raise ValueError(
144	                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
145	                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
146	                )
147	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
148	            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
149	                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
150	                    raise ValueError(
151	                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
152	                        f"{token_id}."
153	                    )
154	
155	        grammars = [
156	            self.json_schema,
157	            self.regex,
158	            self.ebnf,
159	        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
160	        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
161	            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")

163	    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
164	        # Process stop strings
165	        if self.stop_strs is None:
166	            self.stop_strs = []
167	            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
168	        else:
169	            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
170	                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]
171	
172	            stop_str_max_len = 0
173	            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
174	                if tokenizer is not None:
175	                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
176	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
177	                else:
178	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
179	            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len
180	
181	        # Process stop regex strings
182	        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
183	            self.stop_regex_strs = []
184	            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
185	        else:
186	            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
187	                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]
188	
189	            stop_regex_max_len = 0
190	            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
191	                stop_regex_max_len = max(
192	                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
193	                )
194	
195	            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len

198	# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
199	# to be buffered to match the input regex string
200	# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
201	# token
202	def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
203	    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))
204	
205	
206	MAX_LEN = 2**30
207	
208	
209	def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
210	    total = 0
211	    for token, value in subpattern:
212	        if token in {
213	            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
214	            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
215	            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
216	        }:
217	            total += 1
218	        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
219	            # EG: (a\d+) ->
220	            # [(SUBPATTERN,
221	            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
222	            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
223	            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
224	            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
225	        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
226	            _, branches = value
227	            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
228	        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
229	            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
230	            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
231	                total += MAX_LEN
232	            else:
233	                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
234	        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
235	            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
236	            # length
237	            total += 0
238	        else:
239	            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")
240	
241	            total += MAX_LEN
242	
243	    return total

21	@dataclasses.dataclass
22	class SamplingBatchInfo:
23	    # Basic batched sampling params
24	    temperatures: torch.Tensor
25	    top_ps: torch.Tensor
26	    top_ks: torch.Tensor
27	    min_ps: torch.Tensor
28	
29	    # Whether all requests use greedy sampling
30	    is_all_greedy: bool
31	
32	    # Whether any requests use top_p sampling
33	    need_top_p_sampling: bool
34	
35	    # Whether any requests use top_k sampling
36	    need_top_k_sampling: bool
37	
38	    # Whether any request needs min_p sampling
39	    need_min_p_sampling: bool
40	
41	    # Masking tensors for grammar-guided structured outputs
42	    vocab_size: int
43	    grammars: Optional[List] = None
44	    vocab_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
45	    apply_mask_func: Optional[Callable[[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor], None]] = None
46	
47	    # Penalizer
48	    penalizer_orchestrator: Optional[penaltylib.BatchedPenalizerOrchestrator] = None
49	    acc_linear_penalties: torch.Tensor = None  # Used in the overlap mode
50	
51	    # Whether any request has custom logit processor
52	    has_custom_logit_processor: bool = False
53	    # Custom parameters
54	    custom_params: Optional[List[Optional[Dict[str, Any]]]] = None
55	    # Custom logit processor
56	    custom_logit_processor: Optional[
57	        Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]
58	    ] = None
59	
60	    # Used for deterministic sampling
61	    sampling_seed: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
62	
63	    # Device
64	    device: str = "cuda"
65	
66	    # Handle logit bias
67	    logit_bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None

69	    @classmethod
70	    def from_schedule_batch(cls, batch: ScheduleBatch, vocab_size: int):
71	        global_server_args = get_global_server_args()
72	        enable_deterministic = global_server_args.enable_deterministic_inference
73	
74	        reqs = batch.reqs
75	        device = batch.device
76	        temperatures = torch.tensor(
77	            [r.sampling_params.temperature for r in reqs],
78	            dtype=torch.float,
79	            device=device,
80	        ).view(-1, 1)
81	        top_ps = torch.tensor(
82	            [r.sampling_params.top_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
83	        )
84	        top_ks = torch.tensor(
85	            [r.sampling_params.top_k for r in reqs], dtype=torch.int32, device=device
86	        )
87	        min_ps = torch.tensor(
88	            [r.sampling_params.min_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
89	        )
90	        sampling_seed = (
91	            torch.tensor(
92	                [
93	                    (
94	                        r.sampling_params.sampling_seed
95	                        if r.sampling_params.sampling_seed is not None
96	                        else 42
97	                    )
98	                    for r in reqs
99	                ],
100	                dtype=torch.int64,
101	                device=device,
102	            )
103	            if enable_deterministic
104	            else None
105	        )
106	
107	        logit_bias = None
108	        if any(r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None for r in reqs):
109	            logit_bias = torch.zeros(len(reqs), vocab_size, device=device)
110	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
111	                if r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None:
112	                    for key, value in r.sampling_params.logit_bias.items():
113	                        logit_bias[i, int(key)] = value
114	
115	        # Check if any request has custom logit processor
116	        has_custom_logit_processor = (
117	            global_server_args.enable_custom_logit_processor
118	            and any(r.custom_logit_processor for r in reqs)  # check the flag first.
119	        )  # then check the requests.
120	
121	        if has_custom_logit_processor:
122	            # Merge the same type of custom logit processors together
123	            processor_dict = {}
124	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
125	                if r.custom_logit_processor is None:
126	                    continue
127	                processor_str = r.custom_logit_processor
128	                if processor_str not in processor_dict:
129	                    processor_dict[processor_str] = []
130	                processor_dict[processor_str].append(i)
131	
132	            merged_custom_logit_processor = {
133	                hash(processor_str): (
134	                    # The deserialized custom logit processor object
135	                    CustomLogitProcessor.from_str(processor_str),
136	                    # The mask tensor for the requests that use this custom logit processor
137	                    torch.zeros(len(reqs), dtype=torch.bool)
138	                    .scatter_(0, torch.tensor(true_indices), True)
139	                    .to(device, non_blocking=True),
140	                )
141	                for processor_str, true_indices in processor_dict.items()
142	            }
143	            custom_params = [r.sampling_params.custom_params for r in reqs]
144	        else:
145	            merged_custom_logit_processor = None
146	            custom_params = None
147	
148	        # Each penalizers will do nothing if they evaluate themselves as not required by looking at
149	        # the sampling_params of the requests (See {_is_required()} of each penalizers). So this
150	        # should not add hefty computation overhead other than simple checks.
151	        #
152	        # While we can choose not to even create the class instances if they are not required, this
153	        # could add additional complexity to the {ScheduleBatch} class, especially we need to
154	        # handle {filter_batch()} and {merge_batch()} cases as well.
155	        penalizer_orchestrator = penaltylib.BatchedPenalizerOrchestrator(
156	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
157	            batch=batch,
158	            penalizers={
159	                penaltylib.BatchedFrequencyPenalizer,
160	                penaltylib.BatchedMinNewTokensPenalizer,
161	                penaltylib.BatchedPresencePenalizer,
162	            },
163	        )
164	
165	        ret = cls(
166	            temperatures=temperatures,
167	            top_ps=top_ps,
168	            top_ks=top_ks,
169	            min_ps=min_ps,
170	            sampling_seed=sampling_seed,
171	            is_all_greedy=all(r.sampling_params.top_k <= 1 for r in reqs),
172	            need_top_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_p != 1.0 for r in reqs),
173	            need_top_k_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_k != TOP_K_ALL for r in reqs),
174	            need_min_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.min_p > 0 for r in reqs),
175	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
176	            penalizer_orchestrator=penalizer_orchestrator,
177	            has_custom_logit_processor=has_custom_logit_processor,
178	            custom_params=custom_params,
179	            custom_logit_processor=merged_custom_logit_processor,
180	            device=device,
181	            logit_bias=logit_bias,
182	        )
183	        ret.adjusted_from_schedule_batch(batch, vocab_size)
184	        return ret
185	
186	    # placeholder for override
187	    def adjusted_from_schedule_batch(self, batch: ScheduleBatch, vocab_size: int):
188	        pass

190	    # placeholder for override
191	    def adjusted_merge_batch(self, other: "SamplingBatchInfo"):
192	        pass

194	    def __len__(self):
195	        return len(self.temperatures)
196	
197	    def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
198	        if not self.grammars:
199	            self.vocab_mask = None
200	            self.apply_mask_func = None

197	    def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
198	        if not self.grammars:
199	            self.vocab_mask = None
200	            self.apply_mask_func = None
201	            return
202	
203	        # Find a grammar from the list
204	        first_grammar = next(grammar for grammar in self.grammars if grammar)
205	
206	        # TODO(lianmin): Maybe we can reuse the existing mask?
207	        self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(
208	            vocab_size=self.vocab_size,
209	            batch_size=len(self.temperatures),
210	            device=self.device,
211	        )
212	        self.apply_mask_func = (
213	            first_grammar.apply_vocab_mask
214	        )  # force to use static method
215	
216	        # Apply the mask
217	        for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
218	            if grammar and not grammar.finished and not grammar.is_terminated():
219	                grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
220	
221	        # Move the mask to the device if needed
222	        self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, self.device)
223	
224	    def update_penalties(self):
225	        if self.penalizer_orchestrator.is_required:
226	            self.acc_linear_penalties = torch.zeros(
227	                (len(self.temperatures), self.vocab_size),
228	                dtype=torch.float32,
229	                device=self.temperatures.device,
230	            )
231	            self.penalizer_orchestrator.apply(self.acc_linear_penalties)
232	        else:
233	            self.acc_linear_penalties = None
234	
235	    def apply_logits_bias(self, logits: torch.Tensor):
236	        if self.acc_linear_penalties is not None:
237	            # Used in the overlap mode
238	            logits.add_(self.acc_linear_penalties)
239	
240	        if self.penalizer_orchestrator and self.penalizer_orchestrator.is_required:
241	            # Used in the non-overlap mode
242	            self.penalizer_orchestrator.apply(logits)
243	
244	        if self.vocab_mask is not None:
245	            self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
246	
247	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
248	            logits.add_(self.logit_bias)
249	
250	    def filter_batch(self, keep_indices: List[int], keep_indices_device: torch.Tensor):
251	        self.penalizer_orchestrator.filter(keep_indices_device)
252	
253	        if self.has_custom_logit_processor:
254	            self._filter_batch_custom_logit_processor(keep_indices, keep_indices_device)
255	
256	        for item in [
257	            "temperatures",
258	            "top_ps",
259	            "top_ks",
260	            "min_ps",
261	            "sampling_seed",
262	        ]:
263	            value = getattr(self, item, None)
264	            if value is not None:
265	                setattr(self, item, value[keep_indices_device])
266	
267	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
268	            self.logit_bias = self.logit_bias[keep_indices_device]
269	
270	    def _filter_batch_custom_logit_processor(
271	        self, keep_indices: List[int], keep_indices_device: torch.Tensor
272	    ):
273	        """Filter the custom logit processor and custom params"""
274	        self.custom_logit_processor = {
275	            k: (p, mask[keep_indices_device])
276	            for k, (p, mask) in self.custom_logit_processor.items()
277	            if torch.any(
278	                mask[keep_indices_device]
279	            )  # ignore the custom logit processor whose mask is all False
280	        }
281	        self.custom_params = [self.custom_params[i] for i in keep_indices]
282	
283	        # If the custom logit processor is an empty dict, set the flag to False,
284	        # and set the custom logit processor and custom params to None.
285	        if len(self.custom_logit_processor) == 0:
286	            self.custom_logit_processor = None
287	            self.custom_params = None
288	            self.has_custom_logit_processor = False
289	
290	    @staticmethod
291	    def merge_custom_logit_processor(
292	        lhs: Optional[Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]],
293	        rhs: Optional[Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]],
294	        bs1: int,
295	        bs2: int,
296	        device: str,
297	    ):
298	        if lhs is None and rhs is None:
299	            return None
300	        lhs, rhs = lhs or {}, rhs or {}
301	
302	        keys = set(lhs.keys()).union(set(rhs.keys()))
303	        merged_dict = {}
304	
305	        for k in keys:
306	            # Get the logit processor object
307	            processor = lhs[k][0] if k in lhs else rhs[k][0]
308	            # Get and merge the mask tensors from the two dicts
309	            left_mask = (
310	                lhs[k][1]
311	                if k in lhs
312	                else torch.zeros(bs1, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
313	            )
314	            right_mask = (
315	                rhs[k][1]
316	                if k in rhs
317	                else torch.zeros(bs2, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
318	            )
319	            merged_dict[k] = (processor, torch.cat([left_mask, right_mask]))
320	
321	            assert merged_dict[k][1].shape[0] == bs1 + bs2, (
322	                f"The batch size of merged mask ({merged_dict[k][1].shape[0]}) does not match "
323	                f"the sum of the batch sizes of the two masks ({bs1 + bs2})"
324	                f"\n{left_mask=}\n{right_mask=}\n{bs1=}\n{bs2=}"
325	                f"\n{lhs=}\n{rhs=}"
326	            )
327	
328	        return merged_dict
329	
330	    def merge_batch(self, other: "SamplingBatchInfo"):
331	        self.penalizer_orchestrator.merge(other.penalizer_orchestrator)
332	
333	        # Merge the custom logit processors and custom params lists
334	        if self.has_custom_logit_processor or other.has_custom_logit_processor:
335	            # Merge the custom logit processors
336	            self.custom_logit_processor = (
337	                SamplingBatchInfo.merge_custom_logit_processor(
338	                    self.custom_logit_processor,
339	                    other.custom_logit_processor,
340	                    len(self),
341	                    len(other),
342	                    self.device,
343	                )
344	            )
345	            # Merge the custom params lists
346	            self.custom_params = self.custom_params or [None] * len(self)
347	            other.custom_params = other.custom_params or [None] * len(other)
348	            self.custom_params.extend(other.custom_params)
349	
350	            # Set the flag to True if any of the two has custom logit processor
351	            self.has_custom_logit_processor = True
352	
353	        # Merge logit bias - note this has to come before the temperatures tensor update! Otherwise will cause crashes.
354	        # See note below on len(self) and len(other).
355	        self.logit_bias = merge_bias_tensor(
356	            self.logit_bias, other.logit_bias, len(self), len(other), self.device, 0.0
357	        )
358	
359	        # Note: because the __len()__ operator is defined on the temperatures tensor,
360	        # please make sure any merge operation with len(self) or len(other) is done before
361	        # the merge operation of the temperatures tensor below.
362	        for item in [
363	            "temperatures",
364	            "top_ps",
365	            "top_ks",
366	            "min_ps",
367	            "sampling_seed",
368	        ]:
369	            self_val = getattr(self, item, None)
370	            other_val = getattr(other, item, None)
371	            if self_val is not None and other_val is not None:
372	                setattr(self, item, torch.cat([self_val, other_val]))
373	
374	        self.is_all_greedy &= other.is_all_greedy
375	        self.need_top_p_sampling |= other.need_top_p_sampling
376	        self.need_top_k_sampling |= other.need_top_k_sampling
377	        self.need_min_p_sampling |= other.need_min_p_sampling
378	
379	        self.adjusted_merge_batch(other)
380	
381	    def copy_for_forward(self):
382	        # Accumulate the penalty into a pre-allocated buffer to get rid of the dependency of `penalizer_orchestrator` later
383	        self.update_penalties()
384	        return dataclasses.replace(self, penalizer_orchestrator=None)

386	
387	def merge_bias_tensor(
388	    lhs: Optional[torch.Tensor],
389	    rhs: Optional[torch.Tensor],
390	    bs1: int,
391	    bs2: int,
392	    device: str,
393	    default: float,
394	):
395	    """Merge two bias tensors for batch merging.
396	
397	    Args:
398	        lhs: Left-hand side tensor
399	        rhs: Right-hand side tensor
400	        bs1: Batch size of left-hand side tensor
401	        bs2: Batch size of right-hand side tensor
402	        device: Device to place the merged tensor on
403	        default: Default value for missing tensor elements
404	
405	    Returns:
406	        Merged tensor or None if both inputs are None
407	    """
408	    if lhs is None and rhs is None:
409	        return None
410	
411	    if lhs is not None and rhs is not None:
412	        return torch.cat([lhs, rhs])
413	    else:
414	        if lhs is not None:
415	            shape, dtype = lhs.shape[1:], lhs.dtype
416	        else:
417	            shape, dtype = rhs.shape[1:], rhs.dtype
418	
419	        if lhs is None:
420	            lhs = torch.empty((bs1, *shape), device=device, dtype=dtype).fill_(default)
421	        if rhs is None:
422	            rhs = torch.empty((bs2, *shape), device=device, dtype=dtype).fill_(default)
423	        return torch.cat([lhs, rhs])

22	"""
23	Store information about requests and batches.
24	
25	The following is the flow of data structures for a batch:
26	
27	ScheduleBatch -> ModelWorkerBatch -> ForwardBatch
28	
29	- ScheduleBatch is managed by `scheduler.py::Scheduler`.
30	  It contains high-level scheduling data. Most of the data is on the CPU.
31	- ModelWorkerBatch is managed by `tp_worker.py::TpModelWorker`.
32	  It is a subset of `ScheduleBatch` that only contains data related to the model forward on GPU.
33	  It will be transformed from CPU scheduler to GPU model runner.
34	- ForwardBatch is managed by `model_runner.py::ModelRunner`.
35	  It contains low-level tensor data. Most of the data consists of GPU tensors.
36	
37	TODO(lmzheng): ModelWorkerBatch seems a bit redundant and we consider removing it in the future.
38	"""

40	class GuidanceGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
41	
42	    def __init__(self, llguidance_tokenizer: LLTokenizer, serialized_grammar: str):
43	        super().__init__()
44	        self.llguidance_tokenizer = llguidance_tokenizer
45	        self.serialized_grammar = serialized_grammar
46	
47	        self.ll_matcher = LLMatcher(
48	            self.llguidance_tokenizer,
49	            self.serialized_grammar,
50	            log_level=int(os.environ.get("LLGUIDANCE_LOG_LEVEL", "1")),
51	        )
52	        self.bitmask = None
53	
54	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
55	        if not self.ll_matcher.consume_token(token):
56	            logger.warning(f"matcher error: {self.ll_matcher.get_error()}")
57	            self.finished = True
58	
59	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
60	        if self.ll_matcher.is_stopped():
61	            self.finished = True
62	
63	        fill_next_token_bitmask(self.ll_matcher, vocab_mask, idx)
64	
65	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
66	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
67	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
68	        if self.bitmask is None or self.bitmask.shape[0] < batch_size:
69	            # only create bitmask when batch gets larger
70	            self.bitmask = allocate_token_bitmask(
71	                batch_size, self.llguidance_tokenizer.vocab_size
72	            )
73	            bitmask = self.bitmask
74	        else:
75	            bitmask = self.bitmask[:batch_size]
76	
77	        return bitmask
78	
79	    @staticmethod
80	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
81	        return vocab_mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
82	
83	    @staticmethod
84	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
85	        apply_token_bitmask_inplace(logits, vocab_mask)
86	
87	    def copy(self):
88	        return GuidanceGrammar(
89	            llguidance_tokenizer=self.llguidance_tokenizer,
90	            serialized_grammar=self.serialized_grammar,
91	        )
92	
93	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
94	        ff_tokens = self.ll_matcher.compute_ff_tokens()
95	        if ff_tokens:
96	            return ff_tokens, ""
97	        else:
98	            return None

109	class GuidanceBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
110	
111	    def __init__(
112	        self,
113	        tokenizer,
114	        any_whitespace: bool = True,
115	        whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None,
116	        n_vocab: Optional[int] = None,
117	    ):
118	        super().__init__()
119	
120	        self.tokenizer = tokenizer
121	        self.any_whitespace = any_whitespace
122	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
123	        self.llguidance_tokenizer = from_tokenizer(self.tokenizer, n_vocab)
124	
125	    def _from_serialized(self, serialized_grammar) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
126	        try:
127	            return GuidanceGrammar(
128	                llguidance_tokenizer=self.llguidance_tokenizer,
129	                serialized_grammar=serialized_grammar,
130	            )
131	        except Exception as e:
132	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid grammar: {serialized_grammar=}, {e=}")
133	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
134	
135	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
136	        try:
137	            serialized_grammar = LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema(
138	                key_string,
139	                defaults={
140	                    "whitespace_flexible": self.any_whitespace,
141	                    "whitespace_pattern": self.whitespace_pattern,
142	                },
143	            )
144	        except Exception as e:
145	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
146	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
147	        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
148	
149	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
150	        serialized_grammar = grammar_from("regex", key_string)
151	        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
152	
153	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
154	        try:
155	            serialized_grammar = grammar_from("ebnf", key_string)
156	            return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
157	        except ValueError as e:
158	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid ebnf: {key_string=}, {e=}")
159	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
160	
161	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
162	        try:
163	            structural_tag = json.loads(key_string)
164	            assert is_legacy_structural_tag(structural_tag)
165	            tags = [
166	                StructTag(
167	                    begin=structure["begin"],
168	                    grammar=structure["schema"],
169	                    end=structure["end"],
170	                    trigger=structural_tag["triggers"][0],  # TODO?
171	                )
172	                for structure in structural_tag["structures"]
173	            ]
174	            g = StructTag.to_grammar(tags)
175	            return self._from_serialized(g)
176	        except Exception as e:
177	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid structural_tag: {key_string=}, {e=}")
178	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
179

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the constraint system. Let me create a final summary document with all the key findings.

## Summary: SGLang Output Constraints Implementation

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here's a comprehensive overview of how output constraints are implemented:

### 1. **User-Facing API (python/sglang/lang/api.py)**

The `gen()` function accepts constraint parameters:
```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    # ... other params ...
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
):
```

These are passed to the `SglGen` IR node which stores them in `SglSamplingParams`.

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504005815-rjdob3/python/sglang/lang/api.py` (lines 75-139)

---

### 2. **Intermediate Representation (python/sglang/lang/ir.py)**

The `SglGen` class stores sampling parameters in a `SglSamplingParams` dataclass:

```python
class SglGen(SglExpr):
    def __init__(
        self,
        name: Optional[str] = None,
        # ... other params ...
        dtype: Optional[type] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        self.sampling_params = SglSamplingParams(
            # ...
            dtype=dtype,
            regex=regex,
            json_schema=json_schema,
```

### 4. **Constraint Backend Architecture**


The backend uses a dispatcher pattern:
```python
def _init_value_dispatch(
    self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
    s = time.perf_counter()
    key_type, key_string = key
    if key_type == "json":
        grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
    elif key_type == "regex":
        grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
    # ... etc
```

Caching is built-in:
```python
def get_cached_or_future_value(
    self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
    value = self.cache.get(key)
    if value:
        copied_value = value.copy()
        copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
        return copied_value, True
    value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
    return value, False
```

```python
GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}

def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func

def create_grammar_backend(
    server_args: ServerArgs,
    tokenizer,
    vocab_size: int,
    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
    name = server_args.grammar_backend
    
    # Custom grammar backend has highest priority
    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](...)
    
    # Default backends
    if name == "outlines":
        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(...)
    elif name == "xgrammar":
        grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(...)
    elif name == "llguidance":
        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(...)
    elif name == "none":
        return None
```

```python
class SamplingBatchInfo:
    grammars: Optional[List] = None
    vocab_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
    apply_mask_func: Optional[Callable[[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor], None]] = None
    
    def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
        if not self.grammars:
            self.vocab_mask = None
            self.apply_mask_func = None
            return
        
        # Find a grammar from the list
        first_grammar = next(grammar for grammar in self.grammars if grammar)
        
        # Allocate vocab mask
        self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(
            vocab_size=self.vocab_size,
            batch_size=len(self.temperatures),
            device=self.device,
        )
        self.apply_mask_func = first_grammar.apply_vocab_mask
        
        # Apply the mask for each request
        for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
            if grammar and not grammar.finished and not grammar.is_terminated():
                grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
        
        # Move the mask to the device if needed
        self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, self.device)
    
    def apply_logits_bias(self, logits: torch.Tensor):
        if self.vocab_mask is not None:
            self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
```

---

## Key Design Patterns

1. **Pluggable Backends**: Multiple constraint backend implementations (Outlines, XGrammar, LLGuidance) can be swapped
2. **Lazy Compilation**: Constraints are compiled in a separate stage with caching
3. **Async Compilation**: Uses ThreadPoolExecutor for non-blocking grammar compilation
4. **Batched Application**: Vocab masks are applied per-batch during sampling
5. **Token-by-Token Acceptance**: Grammars accept tokens sequentially and track state
6. **Vocab Masking**: Constraints mask invalid tokens in logits before sampling

This architecture enables efficient structured output generation with support for multiple constraint types (JSON schemas, regex, EBNF, structural tags) across different backend implementations.

### 6. **Grammar Registration and Creation** (python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py)

```python
GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}

def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func

def create_grammar_backend(
    server_args: ServerArgs,
    tokenizer,
    vocab_size: int,
    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
    name = server_args.grammar_backend
    
    # Custom grammar backend has highest priority
    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](...)
    
    # Default backends
    if name == "outlines":
        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(...)
    elif name == "xgrammar":
        grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(...)
    elif name == "llguidance":
        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(...)
    elif name == "none":
        return None
```

```python
class GrammarManager:
    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
        # Init grammar cache for this request
        if (
            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
        ):
            if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
                key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
            elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
                key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
            # ... etc
            
            value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
                key, req.require_reasoning
            )
            req.grammar = value
```

### 9. **Constraint Application During Sampling** (python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py)

During batch processing, constraints are applied to logits:

```python
class SamplingBatchInfo:
    grammars: Optional[List] = None
    vocab_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
    apply_mask_func: Optional[Callable[[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor], None]] = None
    
    def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
        if not self.grammars:
            self.vocab_mask = None
            self.apply_mask_func = None
            return
        
        # Find a grammar from the list
        first_grammar = next(grammar for grammar in self.grammars if grammar)
        
        # Allocate vocab mask
        self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(
            vocab_size=self.vocab_size,
            batch_size=len(self.temperatures),
            device=self.device,
        )
        self.apply_mask_func = first_grammar.apply_vocab_mask
        
        # Apply the mask for each request
        for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
            if grammar and not grammar.finished and not grammar.is_terminated():
                grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
        
        # Move the mask to the device if needed
        self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, self.device)
    
    def apply_logits_bias(self, logits: torch.Tensor):
        if self.vocab_mask is not None:
            self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
```

```python
class ScheduleBatch:
    def __init__(self, ...):
        # ... init code ...
        self.has_grammar = any(req.grammar for req in reqs)
        # ...
        if self.sampling_info:
            if self.has_grammar:
                self.sampling_info.grammars = [req.grammar for req in self.reqs]
            else:
                self.sampling_info.grammars = None
```

### 11. **OpenAI API Protocol Support** (python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py)

Constraints are exposed in the OpenAI-compatible API:

```python
class CompletionRequest(BaseModel):
    # ... other fields ...
    top_k: int = -1
    min_p: float = 0.0
    min_tokens: int = 0
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None
    regex: Optional[str] = None
    ebnf: Optional[str] = None
```

Also supports `response_format` for JSON schema:

```python
class ResponseFormat(BaseModel):
    type: Literal["text", "json_object", "json_schema"]
    json_schema: Optional[JsonSchemaResponseFormat] = None
```

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504005815-rjdob3/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`

---

## Key Design Patterns

1. **Pluggable Backends**: Multiple constraint backend implementations (Outlines, XGrammar, LLGuidance) can be swapped
2. **Lazy Compilation**: Constraints are compiled in a separate stage with caching
3. **Async Compilation**: Uses ThreadPoolExecutor for non-blocking grammar compilation
4. **Batched Application**: Vocab masks are applied per-batch during sampling
5. **Token-by-Token Acceptance**: Grammars accept tokens sequentially and track state
6. **Vocab Masking**: Constraints mask invalid tokens in logits before sampling

This architecture enables efficient structured output generation with support for multiple constraint types (JSON schemas, regex, EBNF, structural tags) across different backend implementations.

209	def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
210	    total = 0
211	    for token, value in subpattern:
212	        if token in {
213	            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
214	            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
215	            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
216	        }:
217	            total += 1
218	        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
219	            # EG: (a\d+) ->
220	            # [(SUBPATTERN,
221	            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
222	            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
223	            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
224	            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
225	        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
226	            _, branches = value
227	            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
228	        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
229	            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
230	            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
231	                total += MAX_LEN
232	            else:
233	                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
234	        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
235	            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
236	            # length
237	            total += 0
238	        else:
239	            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")
240	
241	            total += MAX_LEN
242	
243	    return total

# limitations under the License.
# ==============================================================================
"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""

import logging
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Union

# sre_parse is deprecated in Python 3.11+, use re._parser instead
try:
    import re._parser as sre_parse
except ImportError:
    import sre_parse  # Python < 3.11

_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class SamplingParams:
    """
    The sampling parameters.

    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
    for the documentation.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        temperature: float = 1.0,
        top_p: float = 1.0,
        top_k: int = -1,
        min_p: float = 0.0,
        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
        n: int = 1,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
        ignore_eos: bool = False,
        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> None:
        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
        self.stop_strs = stop
        if stop_token_ids:
            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
        else:
            self.stop_token_ids = None
        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
        self.temperature = temperature
        self.top_p = top_p
        self.top_k = top_k
        self.min_p = min_p
        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
        self.regex = regex
        self.n = n
        self.json_schema = json_schema
        self.ebnf = ebnf
        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
        self.custom_params = custom_params
        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed

        # Process some special cases
        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
            self.temperature = 1.0
            self.top_k = 1
        if self.top_k == -1:
            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary

    def verify(self, vocab_size):
        if self.temperature < 0.0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
            )
        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
            raise ValueError(
                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
            )
        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
            )
        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
            )
        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
            )
        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
            raise ValueError(
                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
            )
        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
                )
            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
                )
        if self.logit_bias is not None:
            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
                    raise ValueError(
                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
                        f"{token_id}."
                    )

        grammars = [
            self.json_schema,
            self.regex,
            self.ebnf,
        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")

    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
        # Process stop strings
        if self.stop_strs is None:
            self.stop_strs = []
            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
        else:
            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]

            stop_str_max_len = 0
            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
                if tokenizer is not None:
                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
                else:
                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len

        # Process stop regex strings
        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
            self.stop_regex_strs = []
            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
        else:
            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]

            stop_regex_max_len = 0
            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
                stop_regex_max_len = max(
                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
                )

            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len


# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
# to be buffered to match the input regex string
# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
# token
def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))


MAX_LEN = 2**30


def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
    total = 0
    for token, value in subpattern:
        if token in {
            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
        }:
            total += 1
        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
            # EG: (a\d+) ->
            # [(SUBPATTERN,
            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
            _, branches = value
            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
                total += MAX_LEN
            else:
                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
            # length
            total += 0
        else:
            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")

            total += MAX_LEN

    return total

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import time
from concurrent import futures
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, List

import torch

from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
    create_grammar_backend,
)
from sglang.srt.environ import envs

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from sglang.srt.managers.io_struct import AbortReq
    from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_batch import Req
    from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import Scheduler

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class GrammarManager:
    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
        self.scheduler = scheduler
        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
                self.server_args,
                scheduler.tokenizer,
                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
            )
        else:
            self.grammar_backend = None

        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank

        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
        )

    def __len__(self):
        return len(self.grammar_queue)

    def clear(self):
        if self.grammar_backend:
            self.grammar_backend.reset()

    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0

    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
        for req in self.grammar_queue:
            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
                if req.grammar:
                    req.grammar.cancel()
                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")

    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
        # Init grammar cache for this request
        add_to_grammar_queue = False
        if (
            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
        ):
            if self.grammar_backend is None:
                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
            else:
                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)

                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
                    key, req.require_reasoning
                )
                req.grammar = value

                if not cache_hit:
                    req.grammar_key = key
                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
                else:
                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

        if add_to_grammar_queue:
            self.grammar_queue.append(req)

        return add_to_grammar_queue

    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
        """
        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.

        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)

        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
        """
        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()

        # Poll for ready requests
        start_time = time.perf_counter()
        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
                if i in ready_req_idxs:
                    continue

                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
                    continue

                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
                if req.grammar.done():
                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)

            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)

        # Check failed requests
        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
                if (
                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
                ):
                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)

        # Sync ready and failed requests across all ranks
        if self.grammar_sync_size == 1:
            synced_ready_req_idxs = ready_req_idxs
            synced_failed_req_idxs = failed_req_idxs
        else:
            all_gather_output = [None] * self.grammar_sync_size
            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(
                all_gather_output,
                (ready_req_idxs, failed_req_idxs),
                group=self.grammar_sync_group,
            )
            synced_ready_req_idxs = set.intersection(*[x[0] for x in all_gather_output])
            synced_failed_req_idxs = set.union(*[x[1] for x in all_gather_output])

        # Return ready requests
        return_reqs: List[Req] = []
        for i in synced_ready_req_idxs:
            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
            return_reqs.append(req)
            if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
                continue

            req.grammar = req.grammar.result()
            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, req.grammar.copy())
            if req.grammar is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
                error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request: {req.grammar_key=}"
                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

        # Return failed requests
        for i in synced_failed_req_idxs:
            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
            return_reqs.append(req)

            req.grammar.cancel()
            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ)
            error_msg = f"Grammar preprocessing timed out: {req.grammar_key=}"
            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

        # Remove finished requests from grammar_queue
        self.grammar_queue = [
            req
            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue)
            if i not in synced_ready_req_idxs and i not in synced_failed_req_idxs
        ]
        return return_reqs

1	"""The intermediate representation."""
2	
3	import dataclasses
4	import inspect
5	import warnings
6	from typing import List, Optional, Union
7	
8	from sglang.global_config import global_config
9	from sglang.lang.choices import ChoicesSamplingMethod
10	
11	REGEX_INT = r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
12	REGEX_FLOAT = r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
13	REGEX_BOOL = r"(True|False)"
14	REGEX_STR = r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""  # bugs with regex r"\".*\"" in interegular pkg
15	
16	
17	@dataclasses.dataclass
18	class SglSamplingParams:
19	    max_new_tokens: int = 128
20	    min_new_tokens: int = 0
21	    n: int = 1
22	    stop: Union[str, List[str]] = ()
23	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = ()
24	    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = ()
25	    temperature: float = 1.0
26	    top_p: float = 1.0
27	    top_k: int = -1  # -1 means disable
28	    min_p: float = 0.0
29	    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
30	    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
31	    ignore_eos: bool = False
32	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None
33	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = (None,)
34	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = (None,)
35	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = (None,)
36	    json_schema: Optional[str] = None
37	
38	    # for constrained generation, not included in to_xxx_kwargs
39	    dtype: Optional[str] = None
40	    regex: Optional[str] = None
41	
42	    def clone(self):
43	        return SglSamplingParams(
44	            self.max_new_tokens,
45	            self.min_new_tokens,
46	            self.n,
47	            self.stop,
48	            self.stop_token_ids,
49	            self.stop_regex,
50	            self.temperature,
51	            self.top_p,
52	            self.top_k,
53	            self.min_p,
54	            self.frequency_penalty,
55	            self.presence_penalty,
56	            self.ignore_eos,
57	            self.return_logprob,
58	            self.logprob_start_len,
59	            self.top_logprobs_num,
60	            self.return_text_in_logprobs,
61	            self.json_schema,
62	        )
63	
64	    def to_openai_kwargs(self):
65	        # OpenAI does not support top_k, so we drop it here
66	        if self.regex is not None:
67	            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the OpenAI backend.")
68	        return {
69	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
70	            "max_completion_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
71	            "n": self.n,
72	            "stop": self.stop or None,
73	            "temperature": self.temperature,
74	            "top_p": self.top_p,
75	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
76	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
77	        }
78	
79	    def to_vertexai_kwargs(self):
80	        if self.regex is not None:
81	            warnings.warn(
82	                "Regular expression is not supported in the VertexAI backend."
83	            )
84	        return {
85	            "candidate_count": 1,
86	            "max_output_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
87	            "stop_sequences": self.stop,
88	            "temperature": self.temperature,
89	            "top_p": self.top_p,
90	            "top_k": self.top_k if self.top_k > 0 else None,
91	        }
92	
93	    def to_anthropic_kwargs(self):
94	        # Anthropic does not support frequency_penalty or presence_penalty, so we drop it here
95	        if self.regex is not None:
96	            warnings.warn(
97	                "Regular expression is not supported in the Anthropic backend."
98	            )
99	        return {
100	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
101	            "stop_sequences": (
102	                self.stop if isinstance(self.stop, (list, tuple)) else [self.stop]
103	            ),
104	            "temperature": self.temperature,
105	            "top_p": self.top_p,
106	            "top_k": self.top_k,
107	        }
108	
109	    def to_litellm_kwargs(self):
110	        if self.regex is not None:
111	            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the LiteLLM backend.")
112	        return {
113	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
114	            "stop": self.stop or None,
115	            "temperature": self.temperature,
116	            "top_p": self.top_p,
117	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
118	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
119	        }
120	
121	    def to_srt_kwargs(self):
122	        return {
123	            "max_new_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
124	            "min_new_tokens": self.min_new_tokens,
125	            "n": self.n,
126	            "stop": self.stop,
127	            "stop_token_ids": self.stop_token_ids,
128	            "stop_regex": self.stop_regex,
129	            "temperature": self.temperature,
130	            "top_p": self.top_p,
131	            "top_k": self.top_k,
132	            "min_p": self.min_p,
133	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
134	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
135	            "ignore_eos": self.ignore_eos,
136	            "regex": self.regex,
137	            "json_schema": self.json_schema,
138	        }
